title: DraftOrderLineItem.grams field removed in 2026-07
summary: The grams field in DraftOrderLineItem is removed in API version 2026-07. Replace grams with weight for better flexibility and unit support to avoid errors in future queries.

title: Automated testing for Shopify UI extensions with @shopify/ui-extensions-tester
summary: As of API version 2026-04, Shopify provides an official testing library for UI extensions. `@shopify/ui-extensions-tester` lets you write unit tests for extensions on any surface — Checkout, Admin, Customer Accounts, and POS — without a running Shopify host.

title: New CSS variable for mobile safe area insets
summary: New CSS variable `--shopify-safe-area-inset-bottom` prevents content overlap with Shopify Mobile's bottom bar. Automatically adjusts padding; customize fixed elements using this variable. Live April 15, 2026.

title: New discount fields in the Storefront API's cart types
summary: New discount fields in the Storefront API's cart types

title: Shopify Scripts will be deprecated on June 30, 2026
summary: We are enhancing Shopify Functions with new capabilities for discounts, shipping, and payments. These improvements aim to replace the functionalities you previously achieved using Shopify Scripts, allowing you to build custom logic tailored to your unique business needs.
deadlines:
Here are the key dates to keep in mind as we approach the deprecation of Shopify Scripts:
* **April 15, 2026**—Editing and publishing new Shopify Scripts will no longer be possible

title: Shopify AI Toolkit: Connect your AI tools to the Shopify platform
summary:
The Shopify AI Toolkit is now available. With the Toolkit, you can build apps using Shopify's documentation, API schemas, and code validation, and manage your Shopify store through the CLI's store execute capabilities. 

We're also releasing a new plugin to install the Toolkit.

title: `ActionBar` removed on mobile: `TitleBar` primary action now renders as an icon button
summary: The ActionBar is being removed on mobile. The primary action on the TitleBar now displays as an icon button — defaulting to a + (plus) icon if no icon is specified.

title: New retail cash management capabilities
summary: We've rebuilt cash management on Shopify POS from the ground up. The new Admin GraphQL APIs and POS UI extension targets open up cash management data and controls to app developers, enabling custom cash management workflows that were previously impossible to build on Shopify POS.

title: Identifier support added to the productUpdate mutation
summary:
The `productUpdate` mutation now accepts an `identifier` argument, allowing you to look up the product to update by `id`, `handle`, or `customId` instead of passing the product `ID` inside the `input`.

The `id` and `handle` identifiers let you specify a product's global `ID` or `handle` directly.

title: Payment method identifier now required for customerPaymentMethodRemoteCreate
summary: As of API version 2026-07, the payment method identifier field is now required when using the `customerPaymentMethodRemoteCreate` mutation with Stripe, Authorize.net, or Braintree inputs.

title: Multi-channel support for sales channel apps
summary: Sales channel apps can now create and manage multiple channel connections from a single app. A single app can now establish more than one channel on a shop, with a separate specification and/or external account for each connection.

title: Cart and checkout validation adds billing address and PO number error targets
summary: As of API version 2026-04, you can validate billing addresses and purchase order (PO) numbers in Cart and Checkout Validation Functions. We've added billing address and PO Number to the function input graph and introduced new checkout field targets.

title: Metafield translations now available via GraphQL Admin API
summary: The `Metafield` type now includes a `translations` field, enabling you to query localized metafield values directly via the GraphQL Admin API.

title: Add Prerequisites to Product Discount Functions
summary: Discount functions now support prerequisites for product discount candidates, allowing you to define the \

title: Improved user errors on the `fulfillmentOrderMove` mutation
summary:
The `fulfillmentOrderMove` mutation now returns a specific `fulfillmentOrderMoveUserError` type instead of the generic `userError` type.

## What's changing:
The user error response now includes a code field for easier programmatic error handling and identification.

title: Tax Partner apps can now receive cart line properties in tax calculation requests
summary: As of API version 2026-04, tax calculation requests for Tax Partner Apps can now include cart line item properties. These properties are custom name-value pairs that merchants can add to individual line items.

title: Tax summary webhook and calculation requests now include customer and company metafields
summary: As of API version 2026-04, tax calculation requests for tax partner apps now include buyer metafields. These fields provide custom data associated with customers (for D2C orders) and companies (for B2B orders) that may affect tax calculations.

title: Improved user errors on the `fulfillmentOrderCancel` mutation
summary:
The `fulfillmentOrderCancel` mutation now returns a specific `fulfillmentOrderCancelError` type instead of the generic `userError` type.

## What's changing:
The error response now includes a code field for easier programmatic error handling and identification.

title: `LineItem.weight` field now available in public Admin API
summary: Starting in version `2026-07` of the GraphQL Admin API, you can query the `weight` field on the `LineItem` type in the GraphQL Admin API. This field returns a `Weight` object with `value` and `unit`, making it easy to access line item weights without converting from the REST API's `grams` field.

title: Removing deprecated PRIVATE and PUBLIC_READ enums on metaobject definitions
summary:
We are updating the `MetaobjectAdminAccess` enum by deprecating the `PRIVATE` and `PUBLIC_READ` values. These values are now obsolete and are never returned by the API.

This change removes ambiguity, as the deprecated access values do not accurately reflect the intended access behaviors.

title: Role-based access control and org management for partners
summary:
We have revamped the way partner organizations manage users and stores.

* **Role-based Access Control:** Assign roles to users instead of configuring permissions individually.

title: Removing `visible_to_storefront_api` field on metaobject field definitions
summary: The `visibleToStorefrontApi` field is deprecated and removed. Use the `access` field on metaobject definitions.

title: Line item component information now available for draft orders on the Customer Account API
summary: As of Customer Account API version 2026-04, the `DraftOrderLineItem` object includes a new `components` field. This field returns the individual component line items associated with a parent line item.

title: Add Tags to Discounts
summary: Starting in API version 2026-04, discounts support tags allowing you to efficiently label, group, and organize your discounts.

title: The Shopify CLI app release `--force` flag is deprecated and will be removed
summary: We are deprecating the `--force` flag on the `shopify app deploy` and `shopify app release` commands. The flag will be removed in a Shopify CLI release in May 2026.

title: Removing outdated Polaris reference docs
summary: Polaris reference docs now follow the same versioning policy as Shopify's GraphQL APIs: Each stable version is supported for a minimum of 12 months. Older versions continue to work, they just won’t have dedicated docs on Shopify.dev.

title: Removal of pre_tax_price from the Order REST Admin API
summary: The `pre_tax_price` and `pre_tax_price_set` fields on order line items in the REST Admin API are being removed. These fields were previously available to stores with the Avalara AvaTax 1.0 integration enabled, which was deprecated in April 2025.

title: Updated handling of customer data erasure requests with recent orders
summary: As of March 23, 2026, customer data erasure requests will no longer be held in a pending state for 180 days from the date of the customer's most recent order.
deadlines: As of March 23, 2026, customer data erasure requests will no longer be held in a pending state for 180 days from the date of the customer's most recent order

title: Admin intents now support Settings
summary: We're excited to announce that admin intents now support Settings. This new feature allows apps to direct merchants straight to specific **Settings** pages within the Shopify admin.

title: Process refunds only through the original payment processor – Requirement update
summary:
We've updated requirement 1.1.15 for Shopify App Store apps. 

Apps must now process refunds only through the original payment processor. Previously, apps were prohibited from offering methods for processing refunds outside of the original payment processor.
deadlines: This requirement update will be enforced immediately for new app submissions and will take effect on April 22, 2026, for published apps.

title: Expiring offline access tokens required for new public apps as of April 1, 2026
summary: We're updating how public apps handle offline access tokens to enhance merchant data protection. Starting April 1, 2026, all new public apps must request and use expiring offline access tokens.
deadlines: Starting April 1, 2026, all new public apps must request and use expiring offline access tokens

## What apps are affected

- Public apps created on or after April 1, 2026 that call the Admin API

## What apps are not affected

- Public apps created before April 1, 2026
- Custom apps created at any time
- Apps created by merchants either in the Dev Dashboard or in the admin

## Why we’re making

title: Adding barcode support to inventory shipments API
summary: We're adding barcode support to inventory shipments in version `2026-04`, enabling merchants to assign barcodes to shipments for faster receiving workflows.

title: `inventoryTransferDelete` adds `INVALID_STATE` error code for transfers with in-progress product import
summary: Starting in version 2026-07, the `inventoryTransferDelete` mutation will return a new error code: `INVALID_STATE`. This error will be returned when a client attempts to delete an inventory transfer while a product import related to that transfer is in progress.

title: POS UI extensions can now run without network access
summary:
POS UI extensions can now continue running even when a merchant’s device loses network access.

This feature ensures uninterrupted service during connectivity issues.

title: Adding `createdAt` and `updatedAt` fields to `MetaobjectDefinition` objects
summary: The `createdAt` and  `updatedAt` fields will become available on `MetaobjectDefinition` objects in API version `2026-04`

title: Create subscriptions contracts without payment methods
summary: In the API `2026-04` release candidate version, the `paymentMethodId` field is no longer required when creating a subscription contract using the `subscriptionContractAtomicCreate` and `subscriptionContractCreate` mutations.

title: New payment decline codes added to `OrderTransactionErrorCode` enum
summary: We're adding new error codes to the OrderTransactionErrorCode enum to provide more granular detail about the transaction failure. You can use this more detailed response to improve customer interactions.

title: App-owned metaobjects can be used without access scopes
summary: App-owned metaobjects, identified by types such as `$app:example`, including those created using declarative metaobject definitions, can now be utilized by their owning app without requiring any access scopes.

title: Checkout and Customer Account UI extensions available by default in new development shops
summary: We are ending the developer preview for checkout and customer account UI extensions.

title: ShopifyQL returns fields deprecated and replaced with sales reversals fields
summary: In GraphQL Admin API version `2026-04`, several Returns fields in analytics, which can be queried through ShopifyQL, have been deprecated and replaced with renamed versions (e.g.

title: New rejection reason codes in Payments Apps API
summary: The Payments Apps API now provides more granular decline reason codes for rejected payment sessions.

title: `delegateAccessTokenCreate` mutation now returns `expiresIn`
summary: We have introduced an `expiresIn` field to the `DelegateAccessToken` type, which is returned by the `delegateAccessTokenCreate` mutation. This addition allows app developers using delegate tokens to know precisely when their tokens will expire.

title: Clearer standards for app listing images
summary:
We've updated requirement 4.4.4 and added a new requirement 4.4.5 for Shopify App Store apps.

**Requirement 4.4.4 now provides clearer standards for image quality:** images should primarily show your app's actual user interface and features.
deadlines: These requirements will be enforced starting March 26, 2026

title: Making `fieldDefinitions` optional in `metaobjectDefinitionCreate`
summary: Starting in April 2026, the `fieldDefinitions` input argument for the `metaobjectDefinitionCreate` mutation will become optional. This change means that developers will no longer be required to specify `fieldDefinitions` when creating a new metaobject definition.

title: Create unpaid orders from subscription billing attempts
summary: In the API `2026-04` release candidate version, you can now include a new field called `paymentProcessingPolicy` when creating a billing attempt using the `subscriptionBillingAttemptCreate` mutation. A billing attempt is an action to charge a customer based on their subscription.

title: `inventorySetScheduledChanges` mutation is being removed with no replacement
summary: The `inventorySetScheduledChanges` mutation will be removed from the GraphQL Admin API in version `2026-07`. This mutation has no replacement, so any apps currently using it should stop relying on it to avoid disruptions.

title: Shop Minis February 2026 update
Removed Checkout ID from checkout and order webhooks
summary: To simplify and standardize our checkout references, Shopify has removed the following fields from checkout and orders webhooks. Merchants and partners should update their webhook handlers to use the `token` or `checkout_token` fields going forward.

title: New analyticsQueryable capability for Metafield Definitions
summary: The `analyticsQueryable` capability is now available on the `MetafieldDefinition` type in the GraphQL Admin API, enabling you to specify whether a metafield can be queried in Shopify Analytics.

title: Metaobject access in Shopify Functions
summary:
As of GraphQL API version 2026-04, all Shopify Functions can access app-owned metaobject entries.

Metaobjects are now available for every function target. This feature enables you to query additional structured data present in metaobjects, such as tiered pricing and bundles.

title: Bare query strings no longer bust the cache for assets
summary: Asset caching update ends support for bare query strings in URLs for cache busting. Use Liquid filters like `asset_url` to ensure assets refresh correctly and improve load times.

title: Enhanced discounts support in the Shopify Functions Cart
summary:
As of GraphQL API version `2026-04`, Shopify Functions can now access detailed discount information at three levels:

1. **Cart-level**: All discount applications on the cart.
2. **Line item-level**: Discounts allocated to specific line items.
3.

title: Updates effective February 27 to our Partner Program Agreement and API License and Terms of Use
summary:
Effective Friday, February 27, 2026, **Action Required**

We have updated our Partner Program Agreement and API License and Terms of Use. These changes clarify roles, enhance data protection, and support new platform features.
deadlines:
Effective Friday, February 27, 2026, **Action Required**

We have updated our Partner Program Agreement and API License and Terms of Use
By continuing to use Shopify services on or after February 27, 2026, you confirm that you have read, understood, and accepted these new terms

title: Accelerated checkouts now support multiple product variants on the product page
summary: Accelerated checkout now supports adding multiple product variants from the product page.

title: Adding access field to StandardMetaobjectDefinitionTemplate
summary: We are introducing a new `access` field to the `StandardMetaobjectDefinitionTemplate`. This field will display the template's access rules, helping you determine if a specific template is configured for access via the Storefront API.

title: Legacy customer accounts are now deprecated
summary:
Legacy customer accounts are no longer available to new stores and existing stores not using it. Shopify will stop providing feature updates and technical support for this older version. 

A final sunset date for legacy customer accounts will be announced later in 2026.

title: Support for market-aware auth URLs in Customer Account API
summary:
You can now pass `locale` and `region_country` parameters to the Customer Account API authorization endpoint to build         
 market-aware login experiences in headless storefronts.

title: Manage dev previews on your dev store without leaving admin
summary: As of Shopify CLI 3.91, the Dev Console has been updated to show all active `dev` previews and their extensions, right in the admin. You can now see information on the developer who created the preview, and when it was last updated.

title: New APIs to read and write shipping options in delivery profile
summary: New APIs to read and write shipping options in delivery profile

title: Subscriptions APIs: Introduce SubscriptionBillingAttemptState
summary: As of April 2026, you now have access to the `SubscriptionBillingAttemptState` field in the GraphQL Admin API. This new field replaces several loosely-typed nullable fields with a discriminated union pattern, enhancing the API's ergonomics and self-documentation.

title: New rejection reason codes and merchant message added to `verificationSessionReject` mutation
summary: The `verificationSessionReject` mutation in the Payments Apps API now supports two new rejection reason codes and an optional merchant-facing message.

title: The Dev Dashboard is now multi-lingual
summary:
The Dev Dashboard now renders in your preferred language, and is available in all the languages supported by the Shopify admin.

For more information, see Choosing your account language and region.

title: Shopify-account web component for storefronts
summary: Simplify customer account implementation with our new `shopify-account` component. This web component allows customers to sign in and access account navigation menu—all without leaving the storefront.

title: Use the Admin API and bulk operations in Shopify CLI
summary:
Shopify CLI 3.90.1 now features commands that let you execute queries, mutations, and bulk operations right from your terminal.

* `shopify app execute` allows you to run standard Admin API queries and mutations.

title: Improved app logs and monitoring
summary:
We are enhancing app monitoring and logs to provide better visibility and debugging capabilities, starting with webhooks.

**Monitoring Page Improvements**

* Expanded History: Access up to 30 days of webhook data, including successful deliveries, error counts, and p90 response times.

title: Remaining line items weight available for fulfillment orders
summary: In the API 2026-04 release candidate version, a new field, `remainingLineItemsWeight`, has been added to the `FulfillmentOrder` GraphQL type.

title: Added new enum values for ColumnDataType in ShopifyqlTableDataColumn
summary:
We have introduced 3 new enum values to ColumnDataType in ShopifyqlTableDataColumn: 
- `RATING` : Represents a rating value as float.
- `STRING IDENTITY`:  Represents a GID/UUID value.
- `COLOR`:  Represents a color value in HEX format.

title: JSON metafield values limited to 128KB
summary: When using API version 2026-04 or later, JSON type metafield writes will be limited to 128KB. Limits for all other metafield types remain unchanged (current limits). **All apps that use JSON fields (prior to April 1st, 2026), will be grandfathered at the current 2MB limit.

title: Webhook subscriptions now support a name field for identification
summary: You can now assign an optional name to your webhook subscriptions to simplify identification and management. This feature is available for both TOML-based subscriptions and subscriptions created through the Admin API subscriptions created via the GraphQL Admin API.

title: shopify.app.extensions() in App Bridge now supports POS UI extensions
summary:
The `shopify.app.extensions()` method in App Bridge now supports **Point of Sale (POS) UI extensions**, in addition to checkout, customer account, Admin, and Theme extensions.

With this update, your embedded app can query the status of POS UI extensions along with other extension surfaces.

title: Shop Minis January 2026 update
summary: Shop Minis January 2026 updates: Delayed consent popup, curated Explore screen, CLI dashboard command, product list privacy controls, and UGC documentation

title: The `GiftCardConfiguration` object now includes default gift card expiration
summary:
You can now query a merchant's default gift card expiration settings using the GraphQL Admin API.

The new `expirationConfiguration` field on the `GiftCardConfiguration` object returns the merchant's configured expiration settings, including the duration value and time unit (days, months, or years).

title: Automatically copy cart metafields to orders at checkout completion
summary: As of API version `2026-04`, order metafield definitions can copy values from cart metafields to order metafields when an order is created. You can specify which cart metafields should be carried over to orders.

title: Subscriptions APIs: new payment error code and error classification
summary: We’ve added new payment error codes for subscription billing attempts in the GraphQL Admin API. These new error codes provide more granularity and consistency, making payment errors easier to diagnose. They’re currently available in the `unstable` API version, will be released in version `2026-04`.

title: Increasing the app block limit to 30 for theme app extensions
summary: You can now include up to 30 app blocks in a single theme app extension, an increase from the previous limit of 25. This gives you more flexibility to build comprehensive app experiences that integrate seamlessly with themes.

title: Introducing the `tracks_inventory` query filter for products
summary:
As of GraphQL Admin API version `2026-04`, you can filter products based on whether their inventory is tracked using the new `tracks_inventory` Boolean filter.

This is especially useful when working with merchants like dropshippers who might not maintain on-hand inventory.

title: Discouraging use of `receiptJson` on `OrderTransaction` in the GraphQL Admin API
summary:
We’re **discouraging** use of `OrderTransaction.receiptJson`. You should stop relying on `receiptJson` in production apps.

`receiptJson` is **gateway-defined**, **inconsistently shaped**, and may **change without notice**.

title: Enhanced Discount Function configuration with Admin UI extensions
summary: Admin UI extensions for Discount Functions can now interact with the selected discount method and manage discount classes, letting you customize the discount configuration experience directly within the Shopify admin.

title: Events data now limited to one year retention
summary: Access to `events` data older than one year is no longer available via the Admin GraphQL `Event` interface or the Admin REST `events` resource. This update is part of our ongoing efforts to optimize data management and improve system performance.

title: Ensuring POS UI extension stability by hardening callback handling
summary: Starting with `POS 10.19.0`, unhandled errors in extension callbacks trigger an error page instead of failing silently. This change ensures a more stable and predictable experience for merchants and helps developers identify issues proactively.

title: New `article_list` input setting for themes
summary: You can now add an `article_list` input setting to your theme sections and blocks. This new setting type outputs an article picker that lets merchants select multiple published articles, which can be used for creating featured article sections, related content areas, or article galleries.

title: Checkout UI extensions now default to non-blocking behavior
summary: As of January 26, 2026, Checkout UI extensions that support blocking now default to non-blocking behavior. To enable blocking, merchants must explicitly activate the **Allow app to block checkout** setting in the checkout and accounts editor.

title: Let buyers update subscription payment methods without leaving your extension
summary: Buyers can now replace the payment method on their subscription contract without navigating away from your Customer Account UI extension. Simply invoke the intent and the native payment flow handles the rest, no need to store or display cards yourself.

title: More control over app deploys in CI/CD pipelines
summary: As of Shopify CLI 3.89, you now have more control over the types of extension and configuration updates that should be allowed in your automated app releases. This allows developers to be more confident in the safety of CI/CD pipelines and other app deployment automation.

title: POS UI extensions can now use the device camera
summary:
Starting with POS version 10.19.0, POS UI extensions can now access the device camera through a new Camera API. This enables new use cases like scanning IDs, capturing product photos, and attaching images directly within Shopify POS.

For options, usage examples, and best practices, see Camera API.

title: Localization support for POS UI extensions
summary:
Starting in POS 10.19, you can now localize your POS UI extensions for international merchants, staff, and customers. You can

- **Translate extension text**: Define locale files with translation strings that automatically resolve based on customer and shop locales.

title: Migrate metafields with `shopify app import-custom-data-definitions`
summary: Shopify CLI v3.89 introduces `shopify app import-custom-data-definitions` for migrating app metafield/metaobject definitions to TOML, ensuring consistent, version-controlled deployments.

title: Storefront API now returns a specific error code when Cart Transform Functions fail
summary: As of API version 2026-04, the Storefront API will return a `MERCHANDISE_LINE_TRANSFORMERS_RUN_ERROR` cart error code when a Cart Transform Function encounters a runtime error during cart operations such as `cartCreate` or `cartLinesAdd`.

title: shopify.app.extensions() in App Bridge now supports Admin and Theme app extensions
summary:
The `shopify.app.extensions()` method in App Bridge now supports Admin and Theme app extensions, in addition to the previously available checkout and customer account extensions.

With this update, your embedded app can query the status of extensions across all major surfaces.

title: Shop Minis development now requires Partner account permissions
summary: The Shop Minis CLI has been updated to ensure only authorized team members can create and manage Shop Minis. This is done by verifying the necessary permissions within your Partner organization.

title: Deprecating shopify_y and shopify_s cookies for Hydrogen
summary:
Shopify will stop setting the `shopify_y` and `shopify_s` cookies in 2026.

If you use these cookies, your storefront will continue to work normally. However, if you don’t upgrade, visitor and session attribution won't be reliable in in Shopify Analytics.
deadlines:
**For Hydrogen storefronts, upgrade by April 30, 2026.**

What you need to do:

* If you’re using Hydrogen (React Router) deployed to Oxygen: Run the Hydrogen CLI upgrade command

title: Stricter Liquid parsing for themes and theme app extensions
summary: Starting January 13, 2026, Shopify is enforcing stricter Liquid parsing for all themes. This improves code quality, prevents syntax errors, and enables future Liquid enhancements.
deadlines: Starting January 13, 2026, Shopify is enforcing stricter Liquid parsing for all themes

title: Introducing the `orders/link_requested` webhook topic
summary: Starting with API version 2026-01 of the GraphQL Admin API, your app can subscribe to the `orders/link_requested` webhook topic.

title: Standardization of Asset URL Version Parameters
summary: We're updating the format of versioning query parameters appended to store assets such as CSS and JS. Previously, some assets used a bare numeric query parameter, (for example, `?108`), while others used a key-value format with the `v` query parameter.

title: Report Fulfillment Order progress with new fulfillmentOrderReportProgress GraphQL mutation
summary: As of API Version `2026-04` third-party logistics providers (3PLs) and fulfillment apps can now report progress on fulfillment orders with the `fulfillmentOrderReportProgress` mutation.

title: Deprecated `deliveryShippingOriginAssign` mutation
summary: Support for legacy mode profiles in shipping has been discontinued, as this feature has been inactive for some time. Consequently, the GraphQL Admin API is being updated to align with this change.

title: Access transactions on the Return object
summary: You can now access transaction data directly from the `Return` object in the Admin GraphQL API. This new `transactions` connection simplifies your ability to associate payments and refunds with specific returns, removing the need to deduce relationships based on amounts and timestamps.

title: `INVALID_BILLING_ADDRESS`: new processing error for subscription billing attempts
summary:
We've added a new processing error for subscription billing attempts: `INVALID_BILLING_ADDRESS`.

This error is returned when one or more fields in the billing address contain invalid data.

title: Metafield references added to Customer Account API
summary: We have added `metafield.reference` and `metafield.references` to the Customer Account API. This means that reference `Media` types, including MediaImage, GenericFile, Model3d, and Video, now exist on the common query objects that currently support metafields on the Customer Account API.

title: Tax summary webhook now includes merchant business entity information
summary: As of API version 2026-04, the `tax_summaries/create` webhook for Tax Partner Apps now includes merchant business entity information. This addition provides details about the legal entity responsible for transactions, which is crucial for accurate tax calculation and cross-shop entity mapping.

title: Deprecation of `Shop.billingAddress` in favor of `Shop.shopAddress`
summary: As of API version 2026-01, the `shopAddress` field will be introduced to the `Shop` object in the GraphQL Admin API. This new field replaces the now-deprecated `billingAddress` field, which will be removed in a future version.

title: Removing `permitsSkuSharing` field from fulfillment service
summary: As of API version 2026-04, we've removed the `permitsSkuSharing` field from the `FulfillmentService` object and from the `fulfillmentServiceCreate` and `fulfillmentServiceUpdate` mutations. SKU sharing will be enabled by default for all fulfillment services.

title: Storefront API now returns errors when adding a gift card to a cart with missing or invalid recipient details
summary: As of API version `2026-01`, the Storefront API returns a `GIFT_CARD_RECIPIENT_INVALID` error when adding a gift card to a cart with missing or invalid recipient details, such as in this example.

title: Customer marketing URL fields now require write access
summary: Customer-related fields now require `write_customers` scope and `create_and_edit_customers` permission due to security fixes. Update apps to avoid access denied errors.

title: Discount Function support for rejecting discount codes
summary: The `Discount Function API` now supports discount code rejection, allowing apps to conditionally reject discount codes with a custom message.

title: Set and retrieve `processedAt` in the `refundCreate` mutation
summary:
We have introduced a new `processedAt` input field to the `refundCreate` mutation. With this field, you can manage refund timestamps in your records more effectively.

If you don't provide a `processedAt` value, then the current timestamp is used by default.

title: Faster bulk operations
summary:
We've updated bulk operations in the GraphQL Admin API to process large datasets faster.

**What's new:**
Here's what we've done:

- **Support for all mutations**: Previously bulk mutations only supported a limited set of actions.

title: Venmo and PayPal are now treated as separate payment methods
summary: Starting in API version `2026-01`, Venmo and PayPal are treated as separate payment methods when using payment customization functions. Previously, hiding PayPal's express checkout placement would automatically hide Venmo as well.

title: Improved compare and swap inventory updates for the `inventorySetQuantities` mutation
summary:
This changelog relates to concurrency protection features.

We've enhanced the compare-and-swap functionality in the `inventorySetQuantities` mutation to make concurrent inventory updates more intuitive.

title: Subscription billing attempts throttling
summary: Beginning in API version 2026-01, billing attempts will be throttled based on internal trust metrics to prevent abuse. If an attempt is throttled, it will be visible in the new `throttled` error code on the `BillingAttemptUserErrorCode` enum.

title: Binary testing for Shopify Functions
summary:
We've released the `@shopify/shopify-function-test-helpers` package to simplify writing comprehensive integration tests for Shopify Functions using real production data. All function extension templates now include integration tests powered by this package.

1.

title: The Winter '26 Edition is here
summary:
Announcing 150+ updates to Shopify.

See the updates

title: New fields to combine bundle options
summary: In the API `2026-01` release candidate version, we've introduced consolidated options in the GraphQL Admin API. This feature allows you to support merchants by combining options, such as size and length, to streamline the checkout process for customers.

title: Simplified App Store requirements
summary: Preparing your app for review just became more straightforward. We've simplified and updated App Store requirements to be clearer, better organized, and easier to work with throughout the development process.

title: Offline access tokens now support expiry and refresh
summary: Offline access tokens now expire in 60 minutes and include a refresh token for renewal, enhancing security and aligning with OAuth 2.0. Update token management to handle expirations.

title: Removal of payment method requirement on API created Subscription contracts
summary: We've made it possible create subscription contracts without an attached payment method using the GraphQL Admin API. Contracts created through checkout will continue to require a payment method.

title: New compare and swap syntax for the `inventorySetQuantities` mutation
summary:
This changelog is relates to concurrency protection features.

We're making the `changeFromQuantity` field introduced in `2026-01` mandatory and removing the `compareQuantity` and `ignoreCompareQuantity` fields for the `inventorySetQuantities` mutation.

This is a breaking change.

title: New: Open source experimentation platform
summary:
Tangle is a platform-agnostic, open source experimentation platform with a visual editor and content-based caching.  

With Tangle, you can build ML and data pipelines collaboratively, choosing from reusable modules in the component library or adding your own.

Learn more about Tangle.

title: ShopifyQL Python SDK and CLI now available for analytics
summary:
Developers and merchants can now access ShopifyQL data through a dedicated Python SDK and CLI tool designed for analytics workflows.

The ShopifyQL Python package provides a clean, Pythonic interface for ShopifyQL queries while handling GraphQL API complexity behind the scenes.

title: Monitor admin performance issues by region with Web Vitals API
summary: Enhance your app's global admin performance with regional data from the Web Vitals API. Each Web Vitals event now includes a country field with a two-letter ISO country code, allowing you to pinpoint where users encounter performance issues with your app.

title: Revamped POS UI extensions reference
summary:
We've overhauled the Point of Sale (POS) UI extensions reference, making it easier for you to build extensions that integrate into Shopify's Point of Sale interface.

Updates include:

*   **Expanded guidance:** Detailed explanations and real-world use cases help you implement features.

title: Advanced Metafield \u0026 Metaobject Querying
summary: We've expanded the resources that you can query by metafield value to include Companies and Company Locations.

title: Introducing Sidekick app extensions
summary:
Today, we're introducing Sidekick app extensions to give your app new ways to integrate your app with Sidekick, Shopify's AI-enabled commerce assistant. 

### What are Sidekick app extensions?

Your app can connect its data and workflows using app extensions.

title: Order editing new validations and handled `userErrors`
summary: Order editing mutations now include additional validations. When these validations fail, the API returns relevant `userErrors` in the response. These changes apply to all supported API versions.

title: Making idempotency mandatory for inventory adjustments and refund mutations
summary: Making idempotency mandatory for inventory adjustments and refund mutations

title: Concurrency protection features for inventory and refund mutations
summary: This changelog consolidates all updates related to concurrency protection features for inventory and refund mutations.

title: Making `changeFromQuantity` field required (with explicit opt-out)
summary: Making `changeFromQuantity` field required (with explicit opt-out)

title: Multi-environment theme commands in Shopify CLI
summary: You can now run theme commands across multiple environments simultaneously, making it easier to manage themes across development, staging, and production stores in a single operation.

title: InventoryItem.variant field deprecated in favor of InventoryItem.variants connection
summary:
The `InventoryItem.variant` field has been deprecated and will be removed in a future API version. Use the new `InventoryItem.variants` connection instead. 

To learn more, see `InventoryItem` object or `inventoryItem` query in the GraphQL Admin API reference.

title: A more powerful Dev Assistant on shopify.dev
summary: We've overhauled the Dev Assistant to make it a more reliable and comprehensive companion for your development workflow. While the Assistant is already a familiar tool, this update introduces a new engine designed to deliver higher accuracy and broader knowledge across the entire ecosystem.

title: Tax summary webhook and calculation requests now includes shop and presentment currency amount
summary: As of API version `2026-01`, the `tax_summaries/create` webhook and tax calculation requests for Tax Partner Apps include additional fields for currency. These fields provide amounts in both shop currency and presentment currency.

title: Tax summary webhook `created_at` field now returns UTC timezone
summary: The `created_at` field now returns UTC format, improving consistency across systems. Developers should update parsing logic to handle the new format starting January 2026.

title: Shopify Dev MCP now supports POS UI extensions
summary:
The Shopify Dev MCP server now includes support for code generation for POS (Point of Sale) UI extensions.

With this update, you can use the Shopify Dev MCP as a virtual pair programmer within your preferred IDE.

title: New Google Cloud Run deployment tutorial released
summary: We're excited to introduce a new deployment tutorial for Google Cloud Run.

title: Tax webhook summary and calculation requests now use Global IDs
summary: Starting with API version 2026-01, third-party tax apps will receive Global IDs (GIDs) in tax calculation requests and tax summary webhook payloads for all entity references of the summary section. This aligns with how Partners interact with other Shopify APIs.

title: New `changeFromQuantity` field to manage inventory
summary: This changelog relates to concurrency protection features.

title: Deprecation of `OrderTransaction.authorizationCode` in favor of `OrderTransaction.paymentId`
summary: Starting with the `2026-01` API version, the `authorizationCode` field on the `OrderTransaction` object is deprecated. We recommend using the `paymentId` field instead.

title: Protected customer data scopes required for web pixel PII access starting on December 10th
summary: We will be enforcing Shopify's protected customer data policy for all web pixel extensions starting on December 10th, 2025.

title: Adding idempotency for inventory adjustments and refund mutations
summary: Adding idempotency for inventory adjustments and refund mutations

title: tax_summaries/create webhook and taxSummaryCreate mutation now available
summary:
As of the 2026-01 API version, the `tax_summaries/create` webhook and `taxSummaryCreate` mutation are available for Tax Partner Apps.

## What's New

The `taxSummaryCreate` mutation enables apps to request the generation of tax summaries for orders.

title: Carrier Service API now includes order totals and customer tags
summary: The Carrier Service API callback payload now includes order totals and customer tags. This enhancement enables more effective shipping rate calculations by taking into account the order value and customer segments.

title: Order Update Phone Field Public
summary: As of Admin API 2026-01, the `phone` field is now publicly available in the orderUpdate mutation's OrderInput. This field allows developers to update the customer phone number for an order, overwriting the existing phone number.

title: New Queries for Bulk Operations
summary: We've introduced new GraphQL queries to help you retrieve and manage bulk operations in the Admin API.

title: Support metafield and metaobject standards in Declarative Custom Data Definitions
summary:
We now support enabling and referencing standard metafield and metaobject definitions through an app’s TOML file. Key benefits include:

- Standard metafield and metaobject definitions can be enabled through TOML, and managed through the app dev or app deploy command.

title: Built for Shopify apps get priority visibility across the Shopify App Store
summary: Built for Shopify (BFS) apps are now featured more prominently across key App Store surfaces, helping merchants discover them faster.

title: The `cartDiscountCodeUpdate` mutation now requires the `discountCodes` field
summary: As part of the GraphQL Storefront API 2026-01 release, the `cartDiscountCodesUpdate` mutation now requires the `discountCodes` agrument to be included.

title: Conversion tracking fields added to MarketingEngagementCreate
summary: To improve the integration of marketing platforms that track conversions beyond traditional sales metrics, we have added new fields to the `MarketingEngagementCreateInput` and `MarketingEngagement` objects on the `MarketingEngagementCreate` mutation.

title: Bulk operations group objects default changed to `false`
summary:
We have optimized the output of bulk operations in the GraphQL Admin API to enhance speed and reliability.

## What's Changed

### Bulk Queries (`bulkOperationRunQuery`)

The `groupObjects` argument now defaults to `false`.

title: Optional location inputs for inventory transfers
summary:
You can now create inventory transfers without specifying an origin or destination location. 

Previously, the `InventoryTransferCreateAsReadyToShip` mutation required both origin and destination location IDs. 

Now, these inputs are optional.

title: New ACH support for deferred payments
summary: The 2026-01 Admin GraphQL API introduces support for ACH payment methods, enabling merchants to accept and manage bank account-based payments through Shopify Payments.

title: Increased limits in metafield and metaobject definitions
summary:
We've increased the limits for metafield and metaobject definitions, providing you with more flexibility when building custom data structures for your apps and stores.

- **For app developers**: Each app has its own allocation, so apps aren't competing with each other for definition space.

title: New `notify` parameter available on the `storeCreditAccountCredit` mutation
summary: The `storeCreditAccountCredit` mutation now includes an optional `notify` parameter.

title: Updated online store promotion app store requirement
summary:
Effective Wednesday, November 5th, 2025, the following requirement for the online store has been revised:

* Your app must not use theme app extensions or blocks to promote your app, promote related apps, or request reviews.

title: New: Unlisted Product Status
Improved concurrency handling in the Cart AJAX API and Storefront Cart GraphQL API
summary: As of October 10, 2025, the  Cart AJAX API and Storefront Cart GQL API now processes simultaneous requests. For example, when sending two cart `add` requests at the same time, the Cart AJAX API executes both requests.

title: New Return Reason Definitions API for Better Return Insights
summary: You can now use the new `ReturnReasonDefinition` type to capture more granular, category-specific return reasons when managing returns.

title: The product variant limit is now 2048 for all merchants
summary: It is now possible for all Shopify merchants to create products with up to 2,048 variants in Shopify, exceeding our historical variant limit of 100.

title: POS UI extensions: Developer experience Improvements
summary:
We’ve released a major update to POS UI extensions, aimed at reducing friction and speeding up your workflow. 

Key improvements include:

* **Hot reloading improvements:** We've made hot reloading faster and smoother. Code changes appear on your POS test device instantly without flickering.

title: Introducing the Shop Minis SDK (early access)
summary: Shop Minis offer immersive, full-screen buyer experiences within the Shop app. These experiences are built using familiar web technologies, leveraging our React SDK to streamline development.

title: Subscription Selling Plan support with POS UI Extensions
summary: POS UI Extensions 2025-10 introduces APIs for managing subscription selling plans in Shopify POS.

title: Fulfillment service callback url is now optional
summary: As of the Admin API version 2026-01, the `callbackUrl` argument in the fulfillmentServiceCreate and fulfillmentServiceUpdate mutations is now optional.

title: Deprecate delivery legacy modes fields
summary:
Support for legacy mode profiles in shipping has been discontinued. This feature has been disabled for a while, and the the GraphQL Admin API is being updated to reflect this.

The following fields are being marked as deprecated:

* `DeliveryProfile.

title: Integrate accelerated checkout buttons (Shop Pay and Apple Pay) on mobile app storefronts
summary: Add Shop Pay and Apple Pay checkout buttons to product and cart pages using Checkout Kit for Swift or React Native. Give customers the quick payment options they expect, with just a few lines of code.

title: Themes now use one industry tag for better search results
summary:
The Theme Store has simplified its categorization process by assigning only one industry tag per theme, effective immediately. As a result, all secondary industry tags have been automatically removed.

Your theme's primary industry tag now serves as its sole industry classification.

title: `productSet` mutation now validates `inventoryQuantities` limits
summary: Input contains 51200 inventory quantities, which exceeds the limit of 50000\

title: New `productNetwork` field on the `Order` object
summary: We're adding a `productNetwork` Boolean field on the GraphQL Admin API's `Order` object in version `2025-10`.  This field indicates whether a customer also purchased items from other stores.

title: Polaris unified web components are now stable
summary: Polaris web components are now generally available for extensions with version `2025-10`. This unified toolkit now includes support for POS, Admin, Checkout, and Customer Accounts, and is always up-to-date through Shopify's CDN.

title: Subscription contract shipping requirements now sync with product variants
summary: We've improved how subscription contracts handle shipping requirements to ensure accurate tax calculations, delivery details, and order totals. These changes apply when using the `subscriptionBillingAttemptCreate` mutation.

title: `multipassIdentifier` field added to GraphQL Admin API
summary: We have introduced the `multipassIdentifier` field to the Admin GraphQL API, achieving feature parity with the REST Admin API. This update ensures that both APIs offer the same capabilities, enhancing consistency and flexibility for developers.

title: Upcoming Markets pricing support for Draft Order checkouts
summary: Starting from October 31 2025, when a customer updates their shipping address during a Draft Order checkout, product prices may change based on the shop's Markets configuration.
deadlines: Starting from October 31 2025, when a customer updates their shipping address during a Draft Order checkout, product prices may change based on the shop's Markets configuration

title: Duplicate themes with the Admin GraphQL API
summary:
Starting in version 2025-10, the GraphQL Admin API includes a `themeDuplicate` mutation to duplicate store themes. The mutation accepts a theme ID and an optional `name` parameter to rename the duplicated theme.

For further details, refer to the `themeDuplicate` reference documentation.

title: Introducing the admin intents API
summary: We're excited to introduce admin intents — a transformative API that enhances app interactions with Shopify's admin interface.

title: Built for Shopify grace period extended for uninstall and app embed requirements
summary:
We have extended the grace period for Built for Shopify (BFS) apps that no longer meet specific automated criteria from immediate to 60 days. This extension applies to the following criteria:  

* Uninstall cleanly using theme app extensions.
* Are embedded within the Shopify admin.

title: Removal of deprecated `Shop.draftOrders` connection in the GraphQL Admin API
summary: We are removing the `Shop.draftOrders` connection, which has already been deprecated for some time. This change is part of our ongoing efforts to streamline and improve the API's efficiency and functionality.

title: New: Support for USDC Credit field in Shopify Payments Payout GraphQL
summary:
As of GraphQL Admin API version **2025-10**, `ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSummary` now includes a `usdcRebateCreditAmount` field, which provides a total of all USDC rebate credits issued as part of the payout.

For more information about `ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSummary`, visit the Shopify.dev documentation.

title: New metafield definition types in GraphQL - article_reference / list.article_reference
summary: As of October 2025, Shopify will introduce two new metafield definition types in the Admin GraphQL API, Storefront API, and Liquid. Metafields allow you to customize and store additional information about your Shopify resources, enhancing the flexibility of your online store.

title: Your app may be affected by remote products
summary: Remote products can now be displayed on storefronts. Update Cart Ajax API logic to exclude remote items for shipping, discounts, and recovery. Remote items aren't supported in Shopify Functions.

title: Added support for dispute preventions
App recommendations in Sidekick now available
summary: App discovery and recommendations are now part of Sidekick, allowing merchants to find, compare, and install apps in chat. Sidekick uses App Store listing content to render standardized app cards, reducing steps to install.

title: New balance and due status fields for PaymentSchedule
summary:
You can now access more detailed balance information and payment status updates for payment schedules using deferred payments.

We've introduced three new fields to the PaymentSchedule object:

- **balanceDue**: The remaining balance that needs to be captured for this payment schedule.

title: Dynamic complexity for `productVariantsBulkCreate` and `productVariantsBulkUpdate` mutations
summary: The `productVariantsBulkCreate` and `productVariantsBulkUpdate` mutations now use dynamic complexity costing to more accurately reflect the actual computational cost of operations.

title: ShopifyQL now available as `shopifyqlQuery` within the GraphQL Admin API
summary: Developers can now leverage ShopifyQL to extract valuable insights from all merchant analytics including sales, customer, and product data via the GraphQL Admin API. This enhancement allows for more sophisticated data analysis and reporting.

title: New `mandate` connection added to `CustomerPaymentMethod` object
summary:
As of API version 2025-10, apps with `read_customer_payment_methods` scope can query the new `mandate` connection on the `CustomerPaymentMethod` object. 

This connection returns what each payment method is authorized for (orders, subscriptions, checkout, etc.

title: Order metafield definitions and values can now be used as filters in the Shopify Admin UI
summary: Order metafield definitions and values can now be used as filters in the Shopify Admin UI, enhancing the functionality of the order index.

title: `productVariantsBulkCreate` and `productVariantsBulkUpdate` mutations now validate `inventoryQuantities` limits
summary: Input contains 51200 inventory quantities, which exceeds the limit of 50000\

title: Store credit now supports company locations as account owners in Admin and Customer APIs
summary: As of 2025-10, StoreCreditAccount also support CompanyLocation as owners, enabling location-specific credit management for B2B merchants on the Admin API.

title: Rerouting Fulfillment Orders is now possible via API
summary: We have introduced the `fulfillmentOrdersReroute` mutation to the Admin GraphQL API. This mutation allows you to move fulfillment orders to the next best location based on the shop’s delivery strategies.

title: Dynamic complexity cost for `productCreate` and `productUpdate` mutations
summary: The `productCreate` and `productUpdate` mutations now use dynamic complexity costing to more accurately reflect the actual computational cost of operations.

title: New enum values for FulfillmentEventStatus
summary:
As of version 2025-10, we are introducing a new value to the `FulfillmentEventStatus` enum, to enhance your fulfillment tracking capabilities:

- **CARRIER_PICKED_UP:** This status indicates that the carrier has successfully collected the fulfillment.

title: Facebook (Meta) orders support native exchanges
summary: For Facebook orders created as of August 26, 2025, you can now add `exchangeLineItems` as an input in the `returnCreate` mutation as you do for other orders, eliminating the need for workarounds when handling exchanges for Facebook orders.

title: Metaobject field definitions offer new capabilities
summary: Version 2025-10 allows metaobject fields to include capabilities like `admin_filterable`, enabling filtering in Shopify admin.

title: New components available for Polaris in Admin
summary:
We've updated Polaris with eight new unified components now available for Shopify Admin: 

* `ColorField`
* `ColorPicker`
* `Popover`
* `Tooltip`
* `Menu`
* `Chip`
* `ClickableChip`
* `DropZone`
 
They are already available to you via Shopify's CDN.

title: Upgrade to the latest version of Checkout Kit to avoid issues with iOS 26
summary: Checkout Kit’s preloading feature is not compatible with the upcoming release of iOS 26. This incompatibility will prevent buyers from completing their checkout process within the app.

title: Due on Fulfillment payment term available for pre-orders
summary: In the API `2026-01` release candidate version, a new value, `ON_FULFILLMENT`, has been added to the `SellingPlanRemainingBalanceChargeTrigger` enum. This value allows you to set payments for pre-orders to be processed upon order fulfillment, providing flexibility in managing deferred payments.

title: Setting `permitsSkuSharing` argument to `false` when creating a fulfillment service returns an error
summary: With the 2025-10 API version, the `permitsSkuSharing` argument has been updated for both the `fulfillmentServiceCreate` mutation and the `FulfillmentService#create` REST endpoint to return an error if a `false` value is passed in.

title: Next-Gen developer platform now available to all app developers
summary: Create dev stores on any plan, use app dev preview for instant iteration, manage metaobjects in code, and utilize the new Dev Dashboard for streamlined app management and migration.

title: `@inContext` directive supports visitor consent for privacy-compliant checkouts
summary: The `@inContext` directive now includes a `visitorConsent` parameter, allowing developers to pass buyer consent preferences—such as analytics, preferences, marketing, and sale of data—to cart operations.

title: Global HS code is now supported  in GraphQL Admin API
summary: As of `2025-10`, The `countryCode` field in `CountryHarmonizedSystemCodeInput` is now nullable. When set to `null`, the HS code (harmonized system code) entry represents a global HS code rather than a country-specific one.

title: CollectionReorderProducts operation userErrors now includes code field
summary:
Starting with version 2025-10, the `userErrors` GraphQL type in the GraphQL Admin API's `collectionReorderProducts` mutation will be updated to `CollectionReorderProductsUserError`.

This new type retains all the fields of the previous type and introduces an additional `code` field.

title: Cart mutations now return error for invalid merchandise configuration
summary: We have enhanced error handling for cart mutations to provide clearer feedback when merchandise cannot be added to or maintained in a cart. The cart API now returns a `MERCHANDISE_NOT_APPLICABLE` error code for specific merchandise-related issues.

title: Enhanced API stability with rate limiting for remote payment method creation
summary: The `customerPaymentMethodRemoteCreate` mutation in the Admin API now includes rate limiting to manage excessive API requests and maintain consistent performance for all merchants.

title: Build an Announcement Bar Extension for the Thank You page or Customer Account Pages
summary: We are excited to announce the release of support for building announcement bar extensions on the Thank You page and Customer Account pages, including the Order Status page, Order Index page, and Profile page.

title: Enhanced delivery profile webhooks now include additional payload fields
summary: The `profiles/update` webhook now includes fields like `name`, `default`, `profile_type`, and `version`, improving update reliability and reducing debouncing for rapid profile changes.

title: Storefront API Cart now supports adding Gift Cards
summary:
As of version 2025-10, you can use the GraphQL Storefront API to add gift cards to a cart without replacing gift cards that have already been applied.

After a cart has been created, perform the cartGiftCardCodesAdd mutation to add one or more gift cards to the cart.

title: Determine extension status with the new `shopify.app.extensions()` method in App Bridge
summary:
You can now use the `shopify.app.extensions()` method in App Bridge to identify which of your checkout and customer account extensions are active on a merchant’s store. This feature simplifies tracking setup progress and assists in guiding merchants through onboarding.

With the `shopify.app.

title: New PRESERVE_STANDALONE_VARIANT strategy for the productVariantsBulkCreate mutation
summary: As of GraphQL API version `2025-10`, we have introduced a new value, `PRESERVE_STANDALONE_VARIANT`, to the `ProductVariantsBulkCreateStrategy`. This value can be used as an optional argument in the `productVariantsBulkCreate` mutation.

title: Font library updates: automatic replacement for deprecated fonts
summary: As part of our ongoing move towards open source fonts, we're implementing a gradual automatic font replacement. All deprecated fonts used in online store themes, checkout, and the Shopify Forms app will be automatically replaced with fonts of a similar style.
deadlines: If you're building themes for the Theme Store, you should have already updated your theme presets by the January 3, 2025 deadline

title: Introducing the new `context` field to specify discount eligibility
summary:
As of API version 2025-10, you can use `context` to specify whether code or automatic discounts are eligible for all customers, specific customers, or customer segments. 

Specifying customer eligibility is a new feature for automatic discounts.

title: Introducing `functionHandle` for Shopify Functions
summary: As of the `2025-10` API version, we’re introducing support for user-defined handles as the identifier for Shopify Functions in GraphQL mutations.

title: Order Editing API supports discounts on fulfilled line items
summary: You can now apply discounts to line items that have already been fulfilled using the order editing API, resulting in more flexible order adjustments. The `orderEditAddLineItemDiscount` mutation now supports discount application to both fulfilled and unfulfilled line items.

title: Dynamic complexity cost for `productSet` mutation
summary: The `productSet` mutation now uses dynamic complexity costing that more accurately reflects the actual computational cost of operations.

title: Deprecation of the checkout_and_accounts_configurations/update webhook
summary: The `checkout_and_accounts_configurations/update` webhook, originally introduced in API version 2025-04 to help track updates to checkout and accounts configurations, will be removed on January 1, 2026.
deadlines: The `checkout_and_accounts_configurations/update` webhook, originally introduced in API version 2025-04 to help track updates to checkout and accounts configurations, will be removed on January 1, 2026

title: The `cartDeliveryAddressesUpdate` mutation now supports removing all addresses from a cart
summary:
To clear all delivery addresses associated with a cart, you can now call the `cartDeliveryAddressesUpdate` mutation and set `addresses` to an empty array(`[]`). 

Previously, to clear a cart’s addresses, you could call the `cartBuyerIdentityUpdate` mutation and set `buyerIdentity.

title: `webhookSubscriptionCreate` and `webhookSubscriptionUpdate` support all URI types
summary:
The `webhookSubscriptionCreate` and `webhookSubscriptionUpdate` GraphQL mutations now support a unified `uri` field in the `webhookSubscription` (`WebhookSubscriptionInput`) argument, which accepts:

- HTTPS URLs
- Google Pub/Sub URIs (`pubsub://{project-id}:{topic-id}`)
- Amazon EventBridge event s

title: New: Support for nested cart lines
summary: New fields have been introduced to enable parent-child (nested) relationships between cart lines, supporting use cases such as warranties, engravings, and gift-wrapping.

title: New guidelines for `referenceDocumentUri` in inventory adjustments
summary: We've released new guidelines and updated documentation for the `referenceDocumentUri` field in inventory adjustment mutations. By following these guidelines, your apps can maintain inventory traceability, which helps merchants troubleshoot issues and conduct audits more easily.

title: Support for bulk ad campaign search terms export
summary: Shopify partners can now export search term performance data with detailed targeting breakdowns for multiple ad campaigns simultaneously. Previously, this granular data was only available for one campaign at a time.

title: _tracking_consent, _landing_page, _orig_referrer cookies will no longer be set
summary: Starting Sept 15, 2025, Shopify stops setting `_landing_page`, `_orig_referrer`, `_tracking_consent` cookies. Update code to use browser APIs or Shopify APIs for these values.

title: Request app reviews in admin with the new Reviews API
summary:
The new App Bridge Reviews API allows Shopify apps to request reviews directly within the Shopify admin interface.

- **Better merchant experience:** Leave reviews directly in the Shopify admin with in-context prompts vs. multi-step App Store redirects.

title: Meta Pixel Full Funnel Installation Tracking
summary: We have added support for the Meta Pixel Conversions API, enabling server-side tracking of app installations. This API allows developers to track `Purchase` events when merchants install their apps, enhancing attribution accuracy for Facebook advertising campaigns.

title: Orders query now supports `current_total_price` and `total_weight` filters and `CURRENT_TOTAL_PRICE` as a `sortKey`
summary: The orders `query` argument now supports the `current_total_price` (documentation) and `total_weight` (documentation) filters. Additionally, we've introduced the ability to use `sortKey` with `CURRENT_TOTAL_PRICE` (documentation).

title: Removal of support for the \
MerchandiseSellingPlanNotApplicableOnCompanyLocation warning code for Storefront API
summary: Starting with API version 2025-10 of the GraphQL Storefront API, we have introduced the `SELLING_PLAN_NOT_APPLICABLE_ON_COMPANY_LOCATION` warning code to the  CartWarningCode enum.

title: Deprecation of the `ProductVariant.taxCode` field
summary: As of Admin GraphQL API version `2025-10`, we are deprecating the `taxCode` field from the `ProductVariant` object. This change is part of the Avalara AvaTax app deprecation and will affect how you handle tax classification for product variants.

title: Storefront API Cart now supports replacing Cart delivery addresses
summary:
As of version 2025-10 of the GraphQL Storefront API, you can replace all delivery addresses that are present on a cart in a single operation.

The new `cartDeliveryAddressesReplace`  mutation accepts an `addresses` field for specifying the list of delivery addresses to replace.

title: Analytics API now available for Customer Account UI extensions
summary: Starting from version `2025-07`, Customer Account UI extensions will have the capability to access the analytics object from the Standard API.

title: Hydrogen deploys automatically add preview URLs to Customer Account API application setup
summary: When a Hydrogen branch is deployed, its new preview URL is automatically added to the application setup for the Customer Account API.

title: Web Pixels now run on Customer Accounts and Order Status Page
summary:
Web pixels now automatically load on Customer Account and Order Status pages, enabling you to track the entire customer journey from discovery to order management.

- Shops must use a custom domain (e.g., `accounts.your-store.com`) for customer accounts.

title: Payments App extensions deployment just got faster
summary: You can now deploy payments app extensions instantly without waiting for manual review from Shopify. We've replaced our manual configuration review process with automated validation that gives you immediate feedback directly in your CLI.

title: _shopify_y and _shopify_s cookies will no longer be set
summary: Starting Jan 1, 2026, Shopify stops setting `_shopify_s` and `_shopify_y` cookies. Use Web Pixels API for `_shopify_y` and create session-length cookies for `_shopify_s` replacement.

title: Cart quantity limits are now determined by market region
summary: Cart quantity limits now depend on inventory available in the buyer's market region, not total stock. Previously, customers could add up to the total product inventory from all locations to their cart.

title: Shopify App Store reviews now move between published and archived states based on merchant status
summary:
Our review system continues to evolve to prioritize relevant and trusted reviews. 
* Reviews are archived if they’re left by stores that are on a trial or discounted plan, or that are no longer active.

title: Updates effective July 16 to our Partner Program Agreement and API License and Terms of Use
summary:
EFFECTIVE Wednesday, July 16, 2025 ACTION REQUIRED

We've made changes to our Partner Program Agreement and API License and Terms of Use.
deadlines:
EFFECTIVE Wednesday, July 16, 2025 ACTION REQUIRED

We've made changes to our Partner Program Agreement and API License and Terms of Use
Updates to Revenue Share are effective January 1, 2025
Continued use of Shopify services on or after Wednesday, July 16, 2025 confirms that you’ve read, understood, and accepted these new terms

title: New fields for inventory shipment timestamps
summary: You can now access inventory shipment timestamps through the Admin GraphQL API. You can now use the `dateCreated`, `dateReceived`, and `dateShipped` fields within the `InventoryShipment` type.

title: Improved theme discovery and merchandising on the Shopify Theme Store
summary:
We’ve rolled out several updates to the Shopify Theme Store that help merchants find the right theme faster—while giving your themes more visibility:

**Dedicated theme cards and listing pages for theme presets** 
Each preset now has its own theme card and listing page, providing greater opportunity

title: Sunsetting Flex sections developer preview
summary:
We've closed the Flex sections developer preview as of July 14th. This preview included theme blocks and style settings.

Theme blocks are now available to all theme developers. You can start using theme blocks immediately to create reusable modules for structuring content within sections.

title: Add option to filter by dispute type in OrderListQuery GraphQL query
summary: A new filter called `dispute_type` has been added to the available filters in the Order list (GraphQL query `OrderListData`). It is a multiple choice filter that allows to show only orders that have a `chargeback` and/or an `inquiry` associated with it.

title: The `_ab` cookie will no longer be set
summary: Shopify will stop using the `_ab` cookie for the preview bar on August 18, 2025.

title: Shopify.dev MCP Now Supports More APIs
summary:
The Shopify.dev MCP server now supports code generation for a range of powerful APIs, enhancing your development experience:

- Storefront API
- Partner API
- Customer Account API
- Payment Apps API
- Polaris Web Components
- Liquid

This update allows you to leverage the Shopify.

title: Changes to Cart token format for AJAX and Storefront GraphQL Cart APIs
summary: Cart tokens will now be returned with a new format for both the AJAX and Storefront GraphQL Cart APIs. API features and functionality remain unchanged. Apps and themes should be designed to handle cart tokens in any format and of any length.

title: POS UI Extensions 2025-07 update
Remove unprocessed exchange lines from a return
summary: We're introducing the `removeFromReturn` mutation, which allows you to remove unprocessed items from returns efficiently.

title: New Built for Shopify requirements for marketing apps – Effective July 1, 2025
summary: Starting **July 1, 2025,** new **category-specific requirements apply to marketing apps** in the Built for Shopify (BFS) program.
deadlines: Starting **July 1, 2025,** new **category-specific requirements apply to marketing apps** in the Built for Shopify (BFS) program

title: New warning `DraftOrderMarketRegionCountryCodeNotSupportedWarning` added to `DraftOrder`
summary:
We've added a new new type of warning to the `DraftOrder` object: `DraftOrderMarketRegionCountryCodeNotSupportedWarning`.

The `marketRegionCountryCode` field is deprecated and does not affect draft orders on shops that use Markets.

title: Returns Processing APIs replaces Return Refund APIs
summary:
As announced previously, we're introducing important changes to simplify and enhance return management in the Shopify Admin API.

## What's changing?

- The `returnRefund` mutation is deprecated and replaced by the new `returnProcess` mutation.

title: New AppUninstall mutation to allow apps to uninstall themselves.
summary:
Third-party apps can now uninstall themselves from merchant stores using a new public AppUninstall GraphQL mutation.

The mutation serves as a GraphQL equivalent to the existing REST endpoint for app uninstallation.

For implementation details and examples, please refer to our API documentation.

title: New range fields available for segment filters in GraphQL Admin API
summary: You can now access minimum and maximum range values for segment filters through the GraphQL Admin API. The new `minRange` and `maxRange` fields are available on integer and float segment filters, and event segment filter parameters.

title: New `estimatedShippedAt` argument added to FulfillmentOrderAcceptFulfillmentRequest mutation
summary: As of API version 2025-07, fulfillment service apps can use the new `estimatedShippedAt` argument to specify when they estimate the fulfillment order to become fulfilled. Shopify Fulfillment Network partners will be expected to provide this value when accepting Fulfillment Requests.

title: Increase draft order line item limit from `250` to `499`
summary: In API version `2025-07`, the maximum number of line items accepted by the draft order create and update mutations have been increased from `250` to `499`. This change ensures that the GraphQL API now matches the REST API's limit.

title: Add created/updated at time based filters for an order's fulfillments
summary: As of API version `2025-07` you can now narrow the list of fulfillments returned by `Order.fulfillments` with an optional query argument that targets created_at and/or updated_at fields.

title: Removed tax-related fields from the `ShopFeatures` object
summary: The `EligibleForShopifyTaxReporting` and `ShopifyTaxReportingLegacyAutoTaxMigrated` fields have been removed from the `ShopFeatures` object as it is no longer utilized in Shopify tax reporting. There are no replacements for these fields for external-facing apps.

title: Image alt text can now be translated
summary: Starting with API version 2025-10, image alt text will be exposed as a `TranslatableResourceType`. This means that image alt text surfaced to the buyer will be eligible for translation through the Translations API.

title: Deprecation of POST requests without a Content-Length or Transfer-Encoding: chunked header
summary: We're making changes to our HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 request handling to improve security and compliance with web standards. Starting August 1, 2025, all POST requests to Shopify APIs must include either a Content-Length header or Transfer-Encoding: chunked header, or they'll return an HTTP 411 error.
deadlines: Starting August 1, 2025, all POST requests to Shopify APIs must include either a Content-Length header or Transfer-Encoding: chunked header, or they'll return an HTTP 411 error

title: Add shop_id to app/scopes_update webhook payload
summary: When you update your app's scopes, the `app/scopes_updates` webhook payload now includes the shop ID (`shop_id`).

title: Optional `groupObjects` argument in bulk operations mutations that offers faster and more reliable job execution
summary: We've introduced a new `groupObjects` argument to `bulkOperationRunQuery` and `bulkOperationRunMutation` mutations in the GraphQL Admin API that allow clients optionally to disable grouping and benefit from faster and more reliable bulk operation job runs.

title: Buy Button JS must be upgraded to the latest version
summary: Last year, we announced the deprecation of the Checkout APIs, which Buy Button JS is dependent on. As part of this deprecation, checkout functionality will stop working on older versions of Buy Button JS on August 1, 2025 11:00 AM ET.
deadlines: **Critical Deadline: August 1, 2025 11:00 AM ET

title: Server Pixels: added `subtotal_price` to checkout events
summary: The `subtotal_price` field has been added to Server Pixel events to enhance transaction data tracking.

title: New Liquid filter for displaying unit prices
summary:
You can now use the new `unit_price_with_measurement` filter to display unit prices in the customer's language, enhancing user experience and simplifying theme code. 

Note: Unit prices are available only for stores located in the European Union (EU) or Switzerland.

title: Use `cart.deliveryGroups.groupType` in Function APIs to determine the type of delivery group
summary: With the 2025-07 version release of the Function APIs, you can now use the `cart.deliveryGroups.groupType` to determine the type of delivery group. The `groupType` field indicates whether the delivery is a one-time purchase (`ONE_TIME_PURCHASE`) or part of a recurring subscription (`SUBSCRIPTION`).

title: Shop metafield definitions are now available in the Shopify Admin and Admin API
summary:
Shop metafield definitions and values are now be exposed in the Shopify Admin UI, making them more accessible and manageable by merchants. 

Additionally, it's now possible to query for shop metafield definitions using the `metafieldDefinitions` query in the Admin GraphQL API.

title: Standardized target and operation names across Function APIs
summary: We’ve standardized target and operation names in the Cart Transform, Delivery Customization, Fulfillment Constraints, Order Routing, Payment Customization, Cart and Checkout Validation APIs for better consistency and extensibility.

title: Cart metafields are accessible in Shopify Functions and Checkout UI extensions
summary: You can now read cart metafields in the GraphQL input query for Shopify Functions. Checkout UI extensions can also read and write cart metafields for abandoned carts, order edits, and draft orders, in addition to previously supported resources.

title: Order editing workflows now offer direct session access via mutation arguments and return fields
summary: Order editing workflows now offer direct session access via mutation arguments and return fields

title: Define payment terms conditionally at checkout
summary: You can now set custom payment terms at checkout using the Payment Customization Function. In addition to renaming, reordering, or hiding payment methods, the new `PaymentTermsSetOperation` lets you define fixed, net, or event-based payment terms for each order, with optional deposits.

title: New GraphQL fields for return management
summary:
We're introducing new fields to help you better manage and track returns:

### Quantity tracking fields

The following fields are now available on both `ExchangeLineItem` and `ReturnLineItem` objects:

- `processedQuantity` - Tracks the quantity of a return that has been processed.

title: New pagination limits for Liquid \u0026 Storefront GraphQL API
summary: Liquid and Storefront GraphQL API now limit pagination to 25,000 items, and Liquid page size was increased to 250. Admin API limits are unchanged.

title: The `_shopify_country` cookie will no longer be set
summary: Shopify will no longer set `_shopify_country` on storefronts from Aug 15, 2025. Use Customer Privacy API's `getRegion` method to determine customer location.
