title: `productCreate` surfaces input errors as `userErrors`
summary:
Starting from 2025-07, the following `productCreate` input issues will be surfaced as `userErrors`:

- Linking multiple options to the same metafield
- Linking an option to an already-linked metafield
- Attempting to specify `linkedMetafieldValue` for an option that is not linked to a metafield
- Sp

title: New fields for OrderTransaction and OrderCreateManualPayment
summary: We've added three new fields to the `OrderTransaction` object to give you better insights into order processing: `device`, `location`, and `currency_exchange_adjustment`.

title: New `FREE_GIFT_CARD_NOT_ALLOWED` error code for subscription billing attempts
summary: We’ve added a new error code, `FREE_GIFT_CARD_NOT_ALLOWED`, to the `SubscriptionBillingAttemptErrorCode` enum. This error occurs when a subscription contract includes a gift card product with a purchase price of $0, which is not permitted under Shopify’s business rules.

title: Generally available: Standard product review syndication program
summary:
The standard product review syndication program is now available to all partners, officially entering general availability. 

Any partner can now build a reviews app that syndicates to the standard product review metaobject, as long as their app meets the program requirements.

title: `AMAZON_PAY` and `FACEBOOK_PAY` values enumerated in digital wallets
summary:
The `DigitalWallet` enum type now includes `AMAZON_PAY` and `FACEBOOK_PAY`. For the Storefront and GraphQL Admin QPIs, this update adds Amazon Pay and Facebook Pay (Meta Pay) as enumerated wallets. This change applies to all API versions.

If a query to `shop.paymentDetails.

title: Shopify Payments Payout GraphQL type supports `external_trace_id`
summary:
As of GraphQL Admin API version **2025-10**, ShopifyPaymentsPayout now includes `externalTraceId` field

The `externalTraceId` field provides a unique reference identifier for tracking payouts across external payment systems and financial institutions.

title: Contextual pricing and publishing APIs use backup region fallback
summary: We're changing the fallback behavior when determining the pricing and publishing that applies to a customer in a specific context. Currently, if a country that doesn't belong to a market is passed as a buyer signal, we use the store defaults.

title: Theme files are now installable at the preset level on the Shopify Theme Store
summary:
Installation of individual presets will now match the expectations set by the demo stores in a theme. Previously, only the first preset was installable.

**To enable this:**
1. Submit your theme files that include */listings* folders for approval by the Theme Review Team
2.

title: Add and remove customers from an order with GraphQL
summary: The Admin GraphQL API now includes `orderCustomerRemove` (to remove a customer from an order) and `orderCustomerSet` (to add a customer to an order) mutations, making it easier to manage orders and customers. The `orderCustomerRemove` mutation covers the same use case as setting `order.

title: Shipping Rates – Return backup rates for 3xx and 4xx carrier responses
summary: Carriers occasionally respond with HTTP 3xx redirects or 4xx client errors. Previously, this left merchants without shipping options at checkout. The rate service now extends its existing error-handling path to treat these responses as recoverable failures.

title: The Storefront API's Cart object now exposes warnings for non-applicable discount codes
summary:
As of version 2025-07 of the GraphQL Storefront API, the `Cart` object now provides detailed warnings for non-applicable `CartDiscountCodes`.

Previously, when a discount code could not be applied, the `CartDiscountCode` was returned with `applicable: false`, and no additional details.

title: Deprecation of legacy return APIs and improvements to return management
summary:
We're introducing important changes to simplify and enhance return management in the Shopify Admin API.

## What's changing?

- The `returnRefund` mutation is deprecated and replaced by the new `returnProcess` mutation.

title: New field for discount classes on `DraftOrderPlatformDiscount`
summary: The `discountClass` field in the `DraftOrderPlatformDiscount` object has been deprecated. Please use the `discountClasses` field instead, which allows for the representation of multiple discount classes associated with the backing price rule of the `DraftOrderPlatformDiscount`.

title: Simplified BFS design requirements
summary: Starting June 1st, 2025, apps must meet the simplified BFS design requirements when submitting for review/renewal to be eligible for the BFS program.

title: Improved app recommendations and listing highlights for large merchants
summary:
We’ve improved the recommendation algorithm for large merchants to better surface apps installed and liked by similar-sized businesses. 

In addition to where personalized results are currently shown, the app listing page will have a new section highlighting key insights for large businesses.

title: Shopify Catalog
summary: Shopify Catalog provides select apps and AI agents with access to Shopify’s global product catalog delivering detailed product information including pricing, options, and availability in real-time.

title: Hydrogen May 2025 Release
summary: The latest version of Hydrogen, 2025.5.0 is out today. With this release, Hydrogen is upgrading from Remix 2 to React Router 7. Follow the upgrade guide shared in our Hydrogen May 2025 release blog post https://hydrogen.shopify.dev/update/may-2025.

title: The Summer '25 Edition is here
summary: New features, good vibes. Announcing 150+ updates to Shopify.

title: Recommend theme blocks in the block picker
summary:
You can now recommend specific theme blocks in the block picker, making them easier to find. 

!Block preview

To do this, include the `@theme` block type along with your recommended blocks in the `blocks` array of the schema. All other blocks remain accessible by selecting **Show all**. Learn more.

title: JavaScript and stylesheet tags in snippets
summary: In addition to blocks and sections, you can now use the `{% stylesheet %}` and `{% javascript %}` Liquid tags in snippets. Bundling stylesheet and JavaScript assets helps keep your theme modular.

title: Deprecation of Product, Order, and Shipping Discount Function APIs
summary:
As of API version 2025-04, the following Function APIs are deprecated:

- Product Discount Function API
- Order Discount Function API
- Shipping Discount Function API 
 
**These APIs will be removed from all future stable and unstable API versions.

title: Passing parameters to static blocks
summary: You can now pass arbitraty parameters to static blocks, making it possible simplify the theme code by building more composable components.

title: Hydrogen April 2025 Release
summary: The latest version of Hydrogen v2025.4.0 is out. This release contains updates to internationalization, cart functionality, and template improvements, as well as the latest SFAPI version.

title: Apps now permitted to issue refunds to store credit
summary: As of May 20th, 2025 apps will be permitted to complete refunds to store credit when store credit was not the original payment method. With this update you can issue store credit to customers as a refund and they can later spend that store credit at checkout.

title: Early access: Dev Dashboard
summary: The all new Dev Dashboard serves as a comprehensive hub for all your app development activities, whether you're a merchant creating custom apps or a partner developing public apps.

title: Skeleton theme is now available
summary: The Skeleton theme offers a minimal, modular Shopify template aligned with best practices for quick developer setup.

title: Shopify Functions WebAssembly query API
summary:
Shopify Functions now support a WebAssembly query API, letting you build smaller, faster, and more powerful functions. Deserialize data just-in-time, and only pay for the fields your function actually uses.

Learn more about WebAssembly for Functions.

title: The Shopify.dev MCP server now supports Polaris web components
summary: Polaris web component support is now available in early access on the Shopify Dev MCP server. You'll get up-to-date Polaris API documentation, and integration with LLM-assisted workflows for faster and easier development with the updated components.

title: Improvements to Japanese, Chinese, Korean, and Thai language search on the Shopify App Store
summary: We've optimized Shopify App Store search for Japanese, Chinese, Korean, and Thai, allowing global merchants to discover apps faster and easier.

title: Order cancellation now supports refunds to store credit
summary: As of GraphQL Admin API version `2025-07`, the `orderCancel` mutation allows you to issue refunds as store credit, in addition to the original payment methods, when orders are cancelled.

title: New GraphQL API for Draft Order Delivery Options for Developers
summary: This API enables you to efficiently retrieve all available delivery options for draft orders with a single request. It introduces a specialized endpoint that replaces the legacy `CalculatedDraftOrder` field for available shipping rates.

title: Refund to Store Credit
summary: You can now issue a specified amount as new store credit when processing refunds. This can be done in addition to refunding amounts to the original payment methods.

title: Flow: Improved Send HTTP request action enables secure connections and returns data to the workflow
summary: Shopify Flow has an improved Send HTTP request action that supports a broader range of integrations with external services by securely storing secrets and returning data to subsequent workflow steps.

title: Early access: Declarative Custom Data Definitions
summary:
Declarative custom data definitions offer a streamlined approach for managing and maintaining metafields and metaobjects. Key benefits include:

* Metafield and metaobject definitions are organized in TOML, and managed through the improved `app dev` command.

title: Early access: The re-engineered `shopify app dev`
summary:
The Shopify CLI `app dev` command enables development, preview, and testing of your app on development stores. This command includes a number of improvements when used with early access to the Next-Gen Dev Platform:

* All changes are isolated to your chosen development store.

title: Filter articles by title
summary: We've added a `title` filter to the `articles` query, allowing you to fetch a list of articles by their `title`.

title: Public access for app owned metafields and metaobjects is now disabled
summary:
In line with last year's announcement regarding metafields access simplification, we are now implementing the removal of `PUBLIC` access for app-owned metafields and metaobjects. 

The following updates have been applied across all API versions:

1.

title: Early access: Unified Development Stores
summary: You can now use the new Dev Dashboard to create development stores with any Shopify plan, including Plus. This provides a testing environment that mirrors the features and limitations of specific production plans.

title: Polaris unified web components are now available (early access)
summary: We've updated Polaris, making it a unified web component toolkit for all Shopify surfaces: Admin, Checkout, Customer Accounts. Additionally, Polaris is now smaller, faster, framework-agnostic, and always up-to-date via Shopify’s CDN. This update is in early access.

title: Localhost-based development for `shopify app dev`
summary: Now, you can serve your app using `localhost` (`127.0.0.1`) with a self-signed HTTPS certificate, which Shopify CLI generates for you. This allows you to develop some Shopify app features without the use of network tunnels.

title: Archiving outdated, unhelpful, and untrusted reviews on the Shopify App Store
summary:
To increase the trustworthiness of the Shopify App Store, we’re archiving a significant number of outdated, unhelpful, and untrusted reviews.

Archived reviews are not factored into app ratings or total review counts. Some will remain visible at the end of the reviews section on app listings.

title: Updated Shopify Theme Store Requirements and Submission Process – Effective May 15, 2025
summary:
Starting May 15, 2025, all Shopify themes must meet the updated Theme Store requirements for new submissions and updates.

**Key changes include:**
* **Theme Zip File Structure:** New /listings folder is now required in theme zip files.
deadlines: Starting May 15, 2025, all Shopify themes must meet the updated Theme Store requirements for new submissions and updates

title: Shopify Functions can execute up to 25 functions in a single batch
summary: Shopify Functions now supports up to 25 active functions per Function API, increased from the previous limit of 5. This allows developers to split complex or unrelated logic into smaller, dedicated functions rather than combining everything together.

title: Block previews are now available in theme editor
summary:
Block presets now have visual previews in the picker, similarly to section previews.

!Block preview

No action is required—previews will work automatically.

title: Shopify Functions support in Dev Assistant and the shopify.dev MCP server
summary: We've enhanced the Dev Assistant and the shopify.dev MCP server, adding support for Shopify Functions. Now, AI-powered assistance can help you build and optimize Functions code, streamline your development process, and accelerate your implementation.

title: New reference documentation for Shopify Function APIs
summary:
We've completely revamped our highly-visited and least-loved Shopify Function API reference docs. You’ve shared many helpful comments and questions through our **Was this page helpful?** form, and we’ve reviewed and addressed them in this latest revision.

Here’s the lowdown on the latest updates.

title: POS-specific Shopify Function logic
summary: The new `retailLocation` field in the Function APIs allows you to tailor your logic based on whether a checkout is happening in a retail environment. This allows you to create different behaviors depending on the sales channel or even the specific retail store location.

title: Customer Account API now includes subscription discount data
summary:
The Customer Account API now includes a discounts connection on customer subscription contracts, mirroring functionality that already exists in the Admin API.

What's new:
- Query subscription discount details directly from the Customer Account API.

title: Edit line items with subscriptions and preorders prior to fulfilling an order
summary: You can now edit line items with selling plans (including subscriptions and pre-orders) before an order ships. This update gives you the flexibility to change quantities, add discounts, and remove line items that contain a selling plan.

title: Categories for Section and Block Presets
summary: We've added a new optional `category` property for section and block presets that helps you organize your presets into logical groups. Now your merchants can find the right design elements faster and more intuitively in the Online Store Editor.

title: New Storefront API CartErrorCode: BUYER_CANNOT_PURCHASE_FOR_COMPANY_LOCATION
summary: We've introduced a `BUYER_CANNOT_PURCHASE_FOR_COMPANY_LOCATION` `CartErrorCode`, which indicates a buyer has lost permission to purchase for their selected company location.

title: LiquidDoc for snippets and blocks
summary: LiquidDoc is a new way to add structured documentation directly within your Liquid snippets, and blocks. Define clear input parameters, include helpful descriptions, and provide practical usage examples right alongside your Liquid code.

title: Store credit account balance now available in Liquid
summary: You can now remind customers of their store credit balance by displaying it on the storefront using the new `store credit account object`. This object can be accessed through the `store_credit_account` property of the customer object.

title: Deprecating `gates` types and fields across the GraphQL Admin, Storefront, and Functions APIs
summary: Shopify is retiring gates types and fields due to limited usage and our commitment to enhancing developer tools with more robust APIs. To ensure your applications continue to function optimally, we recommend transitioning your implementations to metafields and metaobjects.

title: Post-purchase extension development without Chrome extension
summary: The `shopify app dev` command now supports post-purchase checkout extensions without requiring the deprecated Chrome extension. This enhancement simplifies development, aligning it more closely with other checkout UI extension patterns.

title: The `UnitPriceMeasurementMeasuredUnit` enum now includes imperial units and counts
summary: In the release candidate for version 2025-07 of the Storefront, GraphQL Admin, and Customer Account API, the  `UnitPriceMeasurementMeasuredUnit` enum now supports values for imperial units and counts.

title: New filter options added to `orders` connection in Customer Account API
summary:
We have added new query parameters to the `orders` connection (`OrderConnection`) within the `customer` `query` of the GraphQL Customer Account API. 

Developers can now filter orders using the `name` and `confirmation_number` parameters, which enables more precise order filtering.

title: Deprecation of `draftOrderCreateMerchantCheckout` mutation
summary:
As of API version 2025-07, the `draftOrderCreateMerchantCheckout` mutation has been deprecated. Please use the `draftOrderComplete` mutation instead.

For more information, please visit our help docs.

title: CustomerPrivacy API \u0026 footer extension target now available for Customer Account extensions
summary: You can now use the CustomerPrivacy API to manage customer privacy on customer account pages, including the **Order status** page.

title: More automated checks for app review pre-submission page
summary: We’ve added new automated checks to the Shopify App Store review process to help you prepare your app for submission, provide faster feedback, and prevent common errors.

title: Web Pixels API: `event.data.checkout.subtotalPrice.amount` value change on the new `/thank-you` page and checkout events
summary:
The `event.data.checkout.subtotalPrice.amount` value in the Web Pixels API has been updated. 

Previously, this value included the sum of all line item prices and product-level discounts, but not order-level discounts. Now, `event.data.checkout.subtotalPrice.

title: Display modals after purchase
summary: The new `paymentSessionModal` mutation raises a modal that will be displayed to the buyer after purchase. This modal uses provided data to display additional info to buyers.

title: Conditional settings in the theme editor
summary: We have introduced a new `visible_if` Liquid setting property that allows you to control the visibility of settings in the Theme Editor. This attribute enables you to hide theme settings that are not currently relevant, while still preserving their data.

title: Update to Shopify’s app developer revenue share
summary: During the pandemic, we lowered our revshare to help small developers and introduced an exemption on the first $1M earned each year. Our ecosystem is stronger than ever—merchants have more than 16,000 apps to choose from, and we paid out more than $1B to developers last year.
deadlines:
**Developers will continue to enjoy a revshare exemption on the first $1 million USD of *lifetime* revenue, and a 15% share on amounts above that.**

Key details:
* Earnings before January 1, 2025 do not count toward the $1 million threshold

title: Cash transaction rounding for Point of Sale purchases in selected countries
summary: For specific currencies, cash transactions on Point of Sale now automatically round to the nearest denomination.

title: Shopify Dev MCP now supports Liquid
summary:
The Shopify Dev MCP server can now search for Liquid docs, and validate Liquid to catch common errors before deployment:

- **Liquid Documentation:** Your AI assistant can now search the complete Liquid API reference, which includes all objects, filters, and tags.

title: New arguments added to `statusPageUrl` field on the `Order` object
summary: We're enhancing the security of order status URLs by replacing static keys with purpose-specific capability tokens. This change helps protect customer data while giving you more control over URL access patterns.

title: [Cart AJAX API] Discounts support on `/cart/update.js`
summary:
We have added support for discounts in the Cart AJAX API's `/cart/update.js` endpoint. You can now add or remove discounts from your cart using the `discount` parameter.

Learn more about cart discount support on shopify.dev.

title: Uncapped counts in GraphQL
summary:
All count APIs in GraphQL now return uncapped results. To retrieve an uncapped count, set the `limit` argument to `null`.

As part of this update, we've standardized the implementation across all GraphQL APIs.

title: Adding `publicDisplayName` field on `ShopPlan`
summary: The `publicDisplayName` field is being added to the `ShopPlan` object. This field provides the publicly displayable name of the shop's current plan when requesting shop properties.

title: Change in `productSet` mutation error code for suspended product
summary:
As of the `2025-07` version of the GraphQL Admin API, an attempt to update a suspended product using `productSet` mutation will now return `PRODUCT_SUSPENDED` error code instead of `INVALID_PRODUCT`.

For more detailed information and examples, visit our `productSet` documentation on Shopify.dev.

title: Payment apps can no longer be embedded in the Shopify admin
summary: As of April 30, 2025, payment apps are no longer eligible to be embedded apps within the Shopify admin. The Payment Apps requirements have been updated to reflect this change.
deadlines: As of April 30, 2025, payment apps are no longer eligible to be embedded apps within the Shopify admin

title: Session creation payloads now include ISO 3166-2 province codes
summary: As of the Payments Apps API version 2025-07, session creation payloads now include a new field for  standardized province codes. For example, `ON` represents Ontario, and `QC` represents Québec. The `province` field, which contains the name of the province as a string, remains in the payload.

title: App ratings now factor in trust signals, with reviews from lower-trust shops carrying less weight.
summary: App star ratings now factor in trust signals alongside recency to provide timely, fair assessments. Reviews from lower-trust shops will carry less weight, and may be unpublished.

title: Storefront API now supports cart attributes with blank keys or values
summary: The GraphQL Storefront API now supports setting cart attributes with either blank keys or values. This change applies to all API versions. This matches the behavior of the AJAX API, which allows for setting blank keys and attributes.

title: New Inventory Transfers experience available for testing in Dev Preview
summary: The Inventory Transfers developer preview increases flexibility for apps that manage multi-location inventory transfers.

title: Allocate a single line item's quantity across multiple fulfillment locations
summary:
Previously, a line item could only be allocated to a single fulfillment location selected by order routing. If the location didn't have sufficient inventory to fulfill all quantities of the line item, then overselling would occur.

Now, a line item can be allocated to multiple fulfillment locations.

title: Developer Preview: Localhost-based development for `shopify app dev`
summary: Now in developer preview, you can serve your app using `localhost` (`127.0.0.1`) with a self-signed HTTPS certificate, which Shopify CLI generates for you. This allows you to develop some Shopify app features without the use of network tunnels.

title: New filter options added to `pages`, `articles`, and `comments` queries
summary:
We've introduced the following new query parameters to the `articles`, `pages`  , and `comments` queries in the GraphQL Admin API:

- You can now filter articles by `blog_id`, `handle`, and `published_at` values
- You can now filter pages by `published_status`, `published_at`, and `id` values
- You

title: View gift card maximum values
summary:
You can now retrieve the maximum values for gift cards using the `giftCardConfiguration` query. There are separate maximum values for purchased and issued gift cards.

Learn more about creating and selling gift cards.

title: New `planHandle` field for managed pricing app subscription plans
summary: App subscription plans created with managed pricing now include plan handle data. This makes it easier and more consistent to query for app subscription plans, because these human-readable handles persist even if the plan ID changes due to updates or edits.

title: Combined Listings Update Mutation Enhancements
summary: As of Admin API 2025-05, we have introduced new error codes that will be returned from the `combinedListingUpdate` mutation. These new errors are to provide more clarity when the mutation is called with incorrect data.

title: New fees and net fields for balance transactions
summary: As of API version 2025-04, you can view the fees and net amount in adjustment orders for balance transactions using the GraphQL Admin API and REST Admin API. These fields are helpful in financial reconciliation, where balance adjustments are used for multiple order transactions.

title: Location ID Queryable with Inventory Scopes
summary:
The access scopes for the `id` field in the Location object have been updated. You can now query this field using either the `read_inventory` or `read_locations` scopes.

Key changes include:
- The `location.

title: Support for card not present transaction details in PaymentsApps API
summary: The Payments Apps API now supports AVS and CVV details for card-not-present transactions.

title: POS UI Extensions - Cart API: Customer fields removed from `subscribable` hook
summary: The POS UI Extensions Cart API has been updated, removing the `email`, `firstName`, `lastName`, and `note` Customer fields from the `subscribable hook`. Any extension used in POS version 10.0.0 or higher, and any extension targeting API version 2025-01 or higher, won't have access to these fields.

title: Shop Component: Shop Pay Payment Request Receipt queries
summary: Shopify now enables developers to retrieve Shop Pay payment request details and statuses directly through GraphQL queries. This feature allows easy access to payment information by specifying a custom `source_identifier` or using the Shop Pay payment request receipt token.

title: App Bridge Title Bar visual update
summary:
Apps will see an improved visual appearance of the App Bridge Title Bar API starting to rollout, to match the new experience in the Shopify admin. 

No developer action is required. The new experience will work with the existing API.

title: Ship and carry out in a single order on POS is now available for Retail Pro Merchants using eligible tax software
summary: Ship and carry out in a single order on POS is now available for Retail Pro Merchants using eligible tax software

title: Hydrogen March 2025 Release
summary:
The latest version of Hydrogen v2025.1.3 is out today. The release contains updates to support Vite 6 and `v3_route_config`:

- Turn on Remix future flag  `v3_routeConfig` (#2722)
- Bump Remix package dependency to 2.16.

title: InventoryItem Webhooks Accessible with Product Scopes
summary: The scopes for receiving webhooks for the InventoryItem object have been relaxed. Webhook topics for this object can now be configured with either the `read_inventory` or `read_products` scope.

title: Shopify.dev MCP Server
summary: We're excited to announce the release of the new Shopify.dev MCP Server! The MCP server gives your AI assistant access to Shopify's development resources, enabling it to search our docs, introspect API schemas, and get up-to-date answers about Shopify APIs.

title: POS UI Extensions 2025-04 Update
summary:
As of April 1, 2025, we made the following updates to POS UI Extensions:

### Features

**Developer Preview**

* Added support for the `pos.transaction-complete.event.observe` target.
* Added support for the `pos.cart-update.event.observe` target.
* Added support for `pos.

title: New field `fulfillmentStatus` added to the `Order` type of the Customer Account API
summary: As of API version 2025-04, we've introduced the field `fulfillmentStatus` to the `Order`object. This field represents the order's aggregated fulfillment status for display purposes.

title: Create smart collections automatically with PRODUCT_CATEGORY_ID_WITH_DESCENDANTS
summary: We've introduced a new `PRODUCT_CATEGORY_ID_WITH_DESCENDANTS` column to the `CollectionRuleColumn`. This field is used for automated collections and allows you to create smart collections based on a new category tree.

title: Liquid support for OKLCH colors
summary:
We've introduced support for OKLCH colors in Liquid, enabling more natural color transitions and access to a broader range of colors than sRGB.

New features include:

- The `color_to_oklch` filter, which takes a `color` object or string and returns an OKLCH color string.

title: New `articleAuthors` query in the GraphQL Admin API
summary: We've added an `articleAuthors` query to the GraphQL Admin API. You can use this query to fetch a list of article authors for a store.

title: Re-enabled customer search terms in the GraphQL and REST Admin APIs
summary: We've re-enabled several customer-related `query` terms in all versions of the GraphQL Admin API and REST Admin API.

title: New additions to delivery promise participants APIs
summary: The ability to use locations as delivery promise participants is currently available in the `unstable` version and is planned for release in the `2025-07` version of the GraphQL Admin API.

title: Storefront API Cart now exposes selling plan errors
summary: As of version 2025-07 of the GraphQL Storefront API, enhanced error handling for selling plans is introduced.

title: Draft Orders automatically purged after 1 year of inactivity
summary: Draft orders created on or after April 1, 2025 will be automatically purged after one year of inactivity, simplifying data management and improving performance for apps interacting with draft orders. Automatic removal of inactive draft orders will begin on April 1, 2026.
deadlines: Draft orders created on or after April 1, 2025 will be automatically purged after one year of inactivity, simplifying data management and improving performance for apps interacting with draft orders
Automatic removal of inactive draft orders will begin on April 1, 2026

title: Using custom ids to look up collections, locations, orders, and product variants
summary:
As of 2025-04 Admin API, you can use your own identifiers to lookup more resource types with the following APIs:

* `collectionByIdentifier`
* `locationByIdentifier`
* `orderByIdentifier`
* `productVariantByIdentifier`

Custom ids are defined by a new metafield type: id.

title: Checkout UI extensions: Attributes API now supports removing cart and checkout attributes
summary: You can now use checkout UI extensions to remove cart and checkout attributes using the **AttributeRemoveChange** property. To remove an attribute during checkout, simply pass the attribute key you wish to remove through this new property.

title: Retail Exchanges now use the Shopify Exchanges implementation
summary: Retail Exchanges are now supported by the Shopify Exchange Platform. Previously, Retail Exchanges used a separate system called ExchangesV2, which handled exchanges as a combination of a Return and an Order Edit, along with any necessary refunds.

title: `draftOrderCount` available in 2025-07
summary: The `draftOrderCount` query is now available in the 2025-07 stable version of the GraphQL Admin API. Previously, it was only available in the `unstable` version.

title: Adding new revocation reasons for payment methods
summary:
We've added the `PAYMENTMETHODVERIFICATION_FAILED` value to the `CustomerPaymentMethodRevocationReason` enum.

`CustomerPaymentMethodRevocationReason` specifies the reasons for revoking customer payment methods.

title: New enum values for `CustomerPaymentMethodRevocationReason`
summary:
We've added the following new values to the `CustomerPaymentMethodRevocationReason` enum:

- `CUSTOMER_REDACTED`: A payment method is redacted for reasons such as GDPR compliance.
- `TOO_MANY_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES`: A payment method has 30 consecutive failures within 35 days.

title: Ads are now available on mobile, plus more surfaces to target merchants
summary: Shopify has introduced new advertising options in the App Store, enabling developers to run ads on mobile devices and target merchants based on location or plan type on the homepage and category pages.

title: FeeSale fee field is nullable as of 2025-07
summary: As of 2025-07, the \u003ccode\u003eFeeSale.fee\u003c/code\u003e field is now nullable, meaning a \u003ccode\u003eFee\u003c/code\u003e can be null if it has been deleted. In versions prior to 2025-07, the \u003ccode\u003efee\u003c/code\u003e field will still return the deleted fee.

title: Deprecating PriceListUserErrorCode values
summary: Starting in API version 2025-04, PriceListErrorCode values that are currently not returned by the API will be hidden.

title: New `include_mirrored_exchanges` query filter parameter
summary: We've added a new `include_mirrored_exchanges` query filter parameter to provide greater flexibility in managing and viewing exchange data using the Retail ExchangeV2 GraphQL Admin API.

title: Now available: JS Buy SDK v3.0
summary: We released the last and final version of JavaScript Buy SDK v3.0 to extend its useful life following the Checkout API deprecation. Upgrading to v3.0 will extend the grace period of SDK's `.
deadlines: **Critical Deadline: July 1, 2025 11:00 AM ET**

title: Stable GraphQL API versions to backfill enum values
summary: Our stable API versions will retroactively add values into GraphQL Enum types, making them non-exhaustive going forward. This assures that new application data values can be represented in older API versions, which provides better stability.

title: Adding defaultPhoneNumber field to Customer
summary:
As of GraphQL Admin API version **2025-04**, the `defaultPhoneNumber` field is introduced on the `Customer` object to support querying a customer's phone number and marketing state.

Learn more about the `Customer` fields on Shopify.dev.

title: Customer RFM group now available in GraphQL Admin API
summary: You can now access a customer's RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) group through the GraphQL Admin API. This new `rfmGroup` field in customer statistics helps you understand customer engagement and purchasing patterns.

title: New developer documentation now available for Shopify Collective
summary: As of March 6, 2025, you can access the new developer documentation for Shopify Collective. This resource is designed to help developers integrate seamlessly with Shopify Collective, especially when working with external dependencies like third-party ERP and PIM solutions.

title: Expose payment detail fields for Payments Apps API
summary: We have introduced new input fields to enhance the capture of payment processing details from providers.

title: New webhook to track updates to checkout and accounts configuration
summary: API version 2025-04 introduces the `checkout_and_accounts_configurations/update` webhook, which fires when merchants update their checkout and accounts configuration (draft and published).
deadlines:
Make sure to subscribe to this webhook as soon as possible, in advance of the upcoming deadlines for merchants to upgrade their Thank you and Order status pages:

- **Plus merchants**: August 28, 2025
- **Non-Plus merchants**: August 26, 2026

title: Support added for app-owned metafields
summary: In the `2025-04` API versions of Checkout and Customer Account UI extension APIs, we've enhanced the `appMetafield` API to support reading app owned metafields. These metafields give you greater control over your application's data, as your app manages both the data and its visibility.

title: `productSet` and `customerSet` mutations now support upserts and custom IDs
summary:
As of version 2025-04, the `productSet` and `customerSet` mutations of the GraphQL Admin API support upserting (creating or updating) records by `identifier`. 

When `identifier` is provided, these mutations use it to check for an existing record.

title: POS UI Extensions: Modal update
summary: In POS version 9.30, the POS UI Extensions modal will be enhanced to prevent accidental dismissals. You won't be able to dismiss the modal by swiping down or tapping outside of it. This update is designed to improve user experience and ensure that important information remains accessible.

title: End of Compatibility for Old POS UI Extensions Versions
summary: Shopify API 2025.04 ends support for specific POS UI Extension versions. Update to the latest version to ensure functionality with POS 9.31 and beyond.

title: [Cart AJAX API] Inventory error message updates
summary: We are updating the error messages in the AJAX API for cases where a client requests more inventory than is available.

title: New GraphQL APIs for Inventory Transfers Management
summary: With the introduction of the new Transfers APIs and webhooks, merchants and developers can now seamlessly integrate inventory transfer data between Shopify and external systems such as Inventory Management Systems (IMS) or Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems.

title: Introducing the .dev Assistant VSCode Extension
summary:
The .dev Assistant extension for VSCode is now deprecated. The extension was a tool to integrate the .dev assistant into your IDE, but our new MCP server solves all the use cases for the extension and more. 

For the latest features and access to Shopify’s development resources, use the Shopify.

title: Checkout APIs will be shut down April 1, 2025
summary: Reminder: The Checkout APIs (Storefront Checkout Mutations and REST Checkout Endpoints) are deprecated and will be shut off on April 1, 2025. Customers will not be able to create or complete checkouts using the deprecated Checkout APIs after the deadline.
deadlines: Reminder: The Checkout APIs (Storefront Checkout Mutations and REST Checkout Endpoints) are deprecated and will be shut off on April 1, 2025
To prevent disruptions, all impacted apps, including mobile apps, need to update to the Storefront Cart API before April 1, 2025

title: Metafield description input field removal
summary:
The `description` field on metafield is being removed from the `MetafieldInput` GraphQL input object. The change will appear in `unstable` and will be included in the `2025-07` API version.

The `description` field is optional and isn't exposed to merchants.

title: New customer address capabilities in the Admin API
summary: Starting with API version 2025-04, we've enhanced the Admin API with new capabilities for managing customer addresses. You can now efficiently create, update, and delete customer addresses using the following mutations: `customerAddressCreate`, `customerAddressUpdate`, and `customerAddressDelete`.

title: The `X-Shopify-API-Deprecated-Reason` HTTP header will return actual GraphQL deprecations if any
summary:
As of `2025-04`, the `X-Shopify-API-Deprecated-Reason` HTTP header will return the list of detected deprecations instead of a generic URL.

**Example**

As of `2025-04`:    `X-Shopify-API-Deprecated-Reason: Shop.products, Shop.

title: Reserved prefix protection for metafields and metaobjects
summary: Starting today, reserved prefixes have been widened to include any phrase. You can no longer make any metafield namespace or metaobject type that includes `--` (e.g., `foo--` , `foo--bar` ).

title: No-op for unchanged metafields and metaobjects
summary: Starting today, when updating metafields and metaobjects, we'll skip triggering webhooks and related actions if the new value matches the existing one. This optimization eliminates unnecessary processing and improves system performance.

title: Hydrogen February 2025 release
summary:
The February 2025 Hydrogen release contains several upgrades:

* Turn on Remix future flag `v3_singleFetch` (#2708)
* Bump Remix package dependency to 2.15.

title: New ends_at, created_at, and updated_at query filter parameters for searching discounts
summary: As of API version 2025-04, we've added new `ends_at`, `created_at`, and `updated_at` filters to the `discountNodes` query, in order to provide greater flexibility in managing and viewing discounts using the GraphQL Admin API.

title: `NON_TEST_ORDER_LIMIT_REACHED` error code for subscriptions billing attempts
summary: We added a `NON_TEST_ORDER_LIMIT_REACHED` field to the `SubscriptionBillingAttemptErrorCode` enum. This indicates that you've reached the order limit with this payment processor, and you'll need to use a test payment gateway to place another order.

title: Updated Country Harmonized System Code validations on Product Variant mutations
summary: Starting with the Admin GraphQL API version 2025-04, all Product Variant mutations will include validations to ensure that any country-specific harmonized system codes provided in the input are compatible with the existing codes on the Inventory Item.

title: Explicit access grants for metafields removed
summary: Completing the transition that began with the deprecation of explicit grants, existing explicit grants in the system will stop working on February 24, 2025.

title: Adding defaultEmailAddress field to Customer
summary:
As of GraphQL Admin API version **2025-04**, the `defaultEmailAddress` field is introduced on the `Customer` object to support querying a customer's email address and marketing state.

Learn more about the `Customer` fields on Shopify.dev.

title: Liquid arrays now support the `find`, `find_index`, `has`, and `reject` filters
summary: We’ve introduced the following new filters to improve how you handle arrays in your Liquid templates. Now, you can quickly retrieve or check for items in an array without writing verbose loops or complex conditional logic.

title: `@inContext` directive added to the Customer Account API
summary: As of API version 2025-04, we've added the `@inContext` directive in the Customer Account API. The `@inContext` directive accepts an argument: `language`.

title: Expanded control of privacy settings using the GraphQL Admin API
summary:
With API version 2025-04, you can manage privacy settings, including status and geotargeting, for first-party features: 

* **Privacy settings status**: View the status of first-party privacy settings, such as the cookie banner, data sale opt-out page, and privacy policy.

title: Discounts reference docs improvements
summary: We’ve rewritten our most-visited and least-loved docs in the **Discounts and marketing** section of the GraphQL Admin API reference. You've shared many helpful comments and questions through our **Was this page helpful?** form, and we’ve reviewed and addressed 100% of them.

title: Attribute Marketing Consent to Retail Locations
summary: You can now attribute customer marketing consent to a specific source location. This enhancement allows you to track marketing opt-in rates by retail location and provides better visibility into how customer marketing opt-ins are captured.

title: Removing unnecessary `RELEVANCE` sort options
Record partial payments on Orders
summary: As of `2025-04` partial payments can be recorded on orders using the new orderCreateManualPayment mutation. This allows the recording of multiple separate payments, up to the total amount owing on the order.

title: Hydrogen January 2025 release
summary:
The January 2025 Hydrogen release contains several upgrades:

* Turn on Remix future flag `v3_lazyRouteDiscovery` (#2702)
* Update SFAPI to 2025-01 (#2715)
* Workaround for “Error: failed to execute ‘insertBefore’ on ‘Node’” that sometimes happen during development (#2701)
* Bump vite, Remix package

title: New Catalog APIs
summary: As of April 2025, the Catalog APIs have been updated to support changes in how markets are managed. For more details, see the New Markets APIs.

title: Flow: Template extensions no longer block deploys
summary: Going forward, when you use `app deploy` to push updated or new Flow template extensions, your app will be deployed immediately. After deployment, Flow will review the template extension. If it is approved, the template will appear in Flow's template library.

title: shop.metaobjects is now just metaobjects in liquid
summary: We've simplified access to metaobjects in Liquid. You can now access metaobjects using the streamlined syntax `metaobjects.type.handle`, aligning with conventions used for other resource types. This new approach is more standardized and is now the preferred method. The previous access syntax, `shop.

title: Payout statuses In Transit and Scheduled have been merged
summary: Payout statuses In Transit and Scheduled have been merged

title: New `event` and `origin` fields for store credit transactions
summary: As of `2025-04`, the `event` and `origin` fields have been added to store credit transactions for the Customer Account GraphQL API.

title: New creation, update, and status filters for subscriptionContracts
summary: As of API version 2025-04, you can now sort the subscriptionContracts query results by `created_at`, `updated_at`, and `status` filters in both the admin and customer account APIs.

title: Apps will be reviewed for necessary scopes
summary:
Starting February 1, 2025, all apps submitted to the Shopify App Store will be checked for scopes that require Shopify permission for approved use cases. 

Apps that have unnecessary scopes, or are using scopes for non-approved use cases will have these scopes removed.
deadlines: Starting February 1, 2025, all apps submitted to the Shopify App Store will be checked for scopes that require Shopify permission for approved use cases

title: Optional `role` argument for `themeCreate` mutation
summary: We've added an optional `role` argument on the `themeCreate` mutation, which allows clients to specify a role for the newly created theme. Only `UNPUBLISHED` and `DEVELOPMENT` roles are permitted for newly created themes.

title: Support added for $app in product queries by metafield
summary: We now support querying products by app-owned metafields with a saved namespace using the `$app` syntax. To include an app-owned metafield in your query, you can use the syntax `metafields.$app.

title: New Buyer Consent Requirement
summary:
As of Tuesday, February 18, 2025, all published apps must display costs and obtain explicit buyer consent before adding any optional paid items to Storefront, Cart, or Checkout. 

These requirements are already being enforced on unpublished apps.
deadlines: As of Tuesday, February 18, 2025, all published apps must display costs and obtain explicit buyer consent before adding any optional paid items to Storefront, Cart, or Checkout

title: Continuous cart authentication
summary: We’ve recently rolled out a change that enables continuous authentication when a customerAccessToken is appended to the cart in the buyerIdentity object. The obtained checkoutUrl allows authenticated customers to navigate to a logged-in checkout experience.

title: New `inventoryItem` field on ProductSetVariantInput
summary:
We are expanding capabilities of the GraphQL Admin API **productSet** mutation by adding  `inventoryItem` field to the **ProductVariantSetInput** type.

This enables `productSet` to modify `ProductVariant` fields related to its `inventoryItem`, such as unit cost and tracked status .

title: InventoryItem Queryable and Updatable with Products Scopes
summary: The scopes for the InventoryItemInput input object and the InventoryItem object have been relaxed. These objects can now be updated and queried using the `write_products` and `read_products` scopes, respectively.

title: Expose the id field in ProductFullSyncPayload object
summary: Version 2025-04: Admin API now includes the return field id in product full sync payload, enhancing data tracking for developers.

title: Line item weight input for `orderCreate` mutation
summary: We have enhanced the `orderCreate` mutation by adding an optional field, `OrderCreateLineItemInput.weight`, which allows you to specify the weight of each line item using the `WeightInput` object.

title: Performance, integration and category-specific requirements come into practice
summary: Performance, integration and category-specific requirements come into practice

title: New card brands for OrderTransactions.paymentMethods
summary: The `OrderTransactions.paymentMethods` enumerated type now includes two new values: `CARTES_BANCAIRES` and `BANCONTACT`. Starting with API version `2025-04`, order transactions paid using these methods will reflect the new values in GraphQL API responses.

title: Shopify Functions template and Shopify CLI changes to support Rust 1.84
summary:
In anticipation of the removal of the wasm32-wasi build target in Rust 1.84, Shopify has updated its Shopify Functions templates for Rust. The `cargo-wasi` utility has been removed because it is incompatible with Rust 1.84 and later versions. 

Additionally, starting with Shopify CLI 3.

title: POS UI Extensions 2025-01 Update
summary:
As of January 20, we added the following updates to POS UI Extensions: 

### Breaking Changes
- Removed the deprecated ActionItem component. Use a Button instead.
- Removed the deprecated SmartGridApi. Use the ActionApi instead.
- Removed the deprecated DiscountType.

title: Add tax validation with localizedFields in Checkout UI Extensions \u0026 Functions
summary: Use the new localizedFields in the Checkout Extensions APIs to apply custom validation for tax fields. The localizationExtensions field in the Admin API has now been renamed to localizedFields.

title: New validations on function input query variables metafields
summary:
As of 2025-01, input query variables metafields will now be subject to additional validation across all Function APIs.

Previously, if these metafields didn't contain properly formatted data, we treated them as empty.

title: New Markets APIs
summary: As of `2025-04`, the Markets APIs have been updated to support additional customizations and conditions for each market, enabling merchants to tailor their shops' buyer experiences.

title: New delivery promise participants API
summary:
As of the 2025-01 Admin GraphQL API, you can now query for delivery promise participants, using `deliveryPromiseParticipants`.

You can make updates using the `deliveryPromiseParticipantsUpdate` mutation.

These APIs control the usage of delivery promises on the platform.

title: Unused PriceListUserErrorCode values removed
summary: Starting in API version 2025-01, PriceListErrorCode values that are currently not returned by the API will be hidden.

title: Introduce concatenatedOriginContract to subscriptionLine
summary:
As of 2025-01, we've introduced `concatenatedOriginContract` to `subscriptionLine` .

You can now query the origin contract of the lines if you have used the Subscription Billing Cycle APIs to combine multiple contracts. This can be accessed through `subscriptionBillingCycle.editedContract.lines.

title: New customer input field for the OrderCreate mutation
summary: As of GraphQL Admin API 2025-01 version, we're deprecating the `customer_id` field in favor of the new `customer` field which you can use to either associate an existing customer or upsert a customer record.

title: Minimum requirement is now optional on automatic discounts
summary: Previously merchants were required to specify minimum purchase conditions on product, order, and free shipping automatic discounts. We're now making these conditions optional, so the  `minimumRequirement` field in our APIs is now able to return a `null` value.

title: Filter products by category or taxonomy metafield
summary:
Starting with Storefront API version 2025-01, you can now filter products within collections and search results using `CategoryFilter` and `TaxonomyMetafieldFilter`.

- **`CategoryFilter`**: Filter products by a category ID.

title: New fields to represent product bundles in a grouped view
summary:
As of API `2025-01` version, we have introduced two new fields that allow you to accurately nest component products under the parent product in a grouped view. 

* The new `components` field has been added to the AbandonedCheckoutLineItem object in the Admin GraphQL API.

title: Admin access input is now optional when setting metafield definition access
summary:
When creating or updating a metafield definition's `access` the `admin` field is now optional. When `admin` is not explicitly set, it will default the same way it is when the entire `access` field is omitted.

Learn more about metafield access controls on Shopify.

title: Public apps must use new GraphQL Product APIs to be accepted in the Shopify App Store
summary:
Starting January 6, 2025, all new apps submitted to the Shopify App Store must use the new GraphQL Product APIs. After this date, apps that query deprecated REST API product resources won’t be approved.

As of February 1, 2025, all public apps must migrate to the new GraphQL Product APIs.
deadlines: Starting January 6, 2025, all new apps submitted to the Shopify App Store must use the new GraphQL Product APIs
As of February 1, 2025, all public apps must migrate to the new GraphQL Product APIs

title: Update in orders webhook to view bundles and their components
summary: Starting with the Admin API version 2025-01, we have added the `sales_line_item_group_id` field to the orders webhook. This field allows you to determine if a specific `line_item` is part of a bundle. When a `line_item` belongs to a bundle, it will have an associated `sales_line_item_group_id`.

title: Deprecation of `storefrontCustomerAccessTokenCreate` Mutation
summary: As of API version `2025-01`, the `storefrontCustomerAccessTokenCreate` mutation is deprecated. This mutation, which was used to exchange the Customer Access Token in the `Authorization` header for a Storefront Customer Access Token, is no longer necessary.
